        • He received this honor posthumously for his actions in preventing a traitor from stealing a fighter plane on August 20, 1971.
        145

        Allama Iqbal did his Bar at Law from ______?

        Correct Answer: Lincoln's Inn University
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Allama Iqbal was awarded his law degree (bar-at-law) from Lincoln's Inn in London.

        He also earned a Ph.D. in philosophy from the University of Munich in 1908.

        146

        Captain Muhammad Sarwar was martyred in the war of _____?

        Correct Answer: 1948
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Captain Muhammad Sarwar Cause of Death: Indo-Pakistani War of 1947–48.

        Captain Raja Muhammad Sarwar Bhatti NH, BS, best known as Muhammad Sarwar, was an officer in the Pakistan Army

        was cited with the first Nishan-i-Haider during the first war between India and Pakistan in 1947–48.

        Raja Muhammad Sarwar Bhatti was born in a small village, Singhori, Gujar Khan, Rawalpindi District, on 10 November 1910,

        Captain Muhammad Sarwar  Died on 27 July 1948 (aged 37) in Uri, Jammu and Kashmir in India

        Captain Muhammad Sarwar was Buried at the Hill of Tilpatra near Uri, Jammu, and Kashmir in India

        agm.04.03.23

        ND17-7-2023


        147

        PILDAT stands for?

        Correct Answer: Pakistan Institute of Legislative Development and Transparency
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan Institute of Legislative Development and Transparency,

        It is a Pakistani think tank that promotes democracy.

        They also organize the Youth Parliament of Pakistan.

        Formation: 2001

        Founder: Ahmed Bilal Mehboob

        Headquarters: Pakistan

        148

        What is the length of Durand line?

        Correct Answer: 2611 Km
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Durand Line is the 2,611-kilometer (1,622-mile) border between Afghanistan and Pakistan

        The common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is called Durand Line

        British signed the Treaty of Durand line with Abur Rehman Khan

        The agreement was signed on November 12, 1893, in Kabul, Afghanistan.

        The agreement was signed between Sir Mortimer Durand, the Indian Foreign Secretary at the time, and Amir Abdur Rahman Khan in Kabul.

        ********

        According to Survey of Pakistan 2020, the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km.

        check here: www.surveyofpakistan.gov.pk

        We often heard Durand Line length is 2252 KM. But at most of the places, we read 2430 KM.

        but right answer is 2611


        149

        Which hospital in Kasur city was renamed as Bulleh Shah Hospital?

        Correct Answer: The District Headquarter (DHQ) Hospital
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        There is a DHQ hospital in Kasur city, which has been named as Bulleh Shah Hospital. 

        150

        Kasur is located in which division?

        Correct Answer: Lahore Division
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Kasur District is a district within the Lahore Division of Punjab

        It was established on 1 July 1976.

        Before its creation, the area comprised two tehsils (Kasur Tehsil and Chunian Tehsil)

        Kasur city, the birth city of the Sufi poet Bulleh Shah

        151

        SNGPL stands for?

        Correct Answer: Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        SNGPL stands for Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited

        It is a Pakistani state-owned gas distribution company 

        It is listed on the Pakistan Stock Exchange.

        Serving more than 7.22 million consumers in north central Pakistan

        Founded: 1963

        Headquarters: Lahore

        Area served: Punjab, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Azad Kashmir & Islamabad

        The company has 16 distribution regions.

        152

        NLC stands for?

        Correct Answer: National Logistics Corporation
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        NLC can stand for National Logistics Corporation, 

        It was formerly known as the National Logistics Cell,

        It is a Pakistani state-owned logistics company based in Rawalpindi,

        Founded July 20, 1978; 47 years ago

        Headquarters Rawalpindi,

        153

        The book The Spread of Islam in the World is written by:

        Correct Answer: Sir Thomas W. Arnoid
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Spread Of Islam In The World by Thomas Walker Arnold.

        The book, which was first published in 1896

        Thomas Walker Arnold CIE FBA was a British orientalist and historian of Islamic art.

        He taught at Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College, later Aligarh Muslim University, and Government College University, Lahore



        154

        Babri mosque which was demolished in 1992 was situated in the province in _____?

        Correct Answer: Utar Pardesh
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Babri Masjid is located in Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh, India

        On 6 December 1992, a large group of Hindu activists of the Vishva Hindu Parishad and allied organizations demolished.

        Babri Masjid Completed in 1528–29

        Babri Masjid Demolished in 1992





        155

        Who wrote the book Yadgar-i-Ghalib?

        Correct Answer: Altaf Husain Hali
        Show explanation
        Explanation

         Yadgar-i-Ghalib is a 1897 biography of the Urdu poet Ghalib,

        He wwas ritten by his disciple and writer/poet Altaf Hussain Hali (1837–1914).

        Publication date 1897

        It is considered to be the first authentic work on Ghalib's life, personality, poetry and prose.

        156

        Khawaja Altaf Hussain Hali was awarded the title of Shamsul Ulama.

        Correct Answer: 1904
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In 1904, Hali was given the title of Shamsul Ulama by the British Government.

        This title was reserved for the highly learned.

        He was an Urdu poet and writer.

        Years active 1860–1914

        Notable works Musaddas-e-Hali

        • Yadgar-i-Ghalib
        • Hayat-i-Saadi
        • Hayat-i-Javed
        157

        Number of members of election commission ?

        Correct Answer: 4
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Election Commission of Pakistan have 4 members panel from all the four provinces .

        4 Provincial Election Commissioners.

        The Commission transacts its business by holding meetings.

        The Chief Election Commissioner have tenure of 5 years.

        The members Election Commissioner have tenure of 5 years.



        158

        APNEC stands for?

        Correct Answer: All Pakistan Newspapers and Electronic Media Employees Confederation
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        APNEC stands for the All Pakistan Newspapers and Electronic Media Employees Confederation.

        It was established in 1974 to protect the rights of both journalistic and non-journalistic staff 

        159

        PICIC stands for_____?

        Correct Answer: Pakistan International Commerce and Investment Council
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        PICIC stands for Pakistan Industrial Credit and Investment Corporation

        It is a financial institution in Pakistan,

        It is one of the first development finance institutions established with the World Bank Group

        Founded: 1957; 68 years ago

        Headquarters Pakistan

        160

        WASA stands for ________?

        Correct Answer: Water and Sanitation Agency
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        WASA stands for Water and Sanitation Agency

        It was established by the Lahore Development Authority (LDA) in 1976

        For the planning, designing, development, and maintenance of water supply, sewerage, and drainage systems in Lahore.

        161

        All India National Congress was formed/established by _____?

        Correct Answer: A.O Hume
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Indian National Congress was founded on 28 December 1885 

        It was founded at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay 

        Its Headquarters is located in New Delhi

        It was founded by Allan Octavian Hume.


        Additional information

        Allan Octavian Hume Place was appointed as the General Secretary,

        Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee of Calcutta was elected the first President.


        162

        Who took oath from PM Liaqat Ali Khan ?

        Correct Answer: Quaid-i-Azam
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Liaqat Ali Khan was named Pakistan’s first Prime Minister by the country’s founding fathers after the country gained independence in 1947.

        Liaqat Ali Khan administered the oath to Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

        ****

        163

        East India Company was established in ______?

        Correct Answer: December 31, 1600
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The East India Company was an English, and later British, joint-stock company

        East India Company was established on December 31, 1600.

        Ceased operations: June 1, 1874

        Headquarters: London, United Kingdom

        Founders: Thomas Smythe, Royal charter, John Watts

        Defunct: 1 June 1874; 149 years ago

        AGM 30.4.23


        164

        Chief of Army Staff General Asim Munir has been promoted to which rank in May 2025?

        Correct Answer: Field Marshal
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        On 20 May 2025, the federal cabinet approved the promotion of Chief of Army Staff (COAS) General Asim Munir to Field Marshal.

        For ensuring the security of the country and defeating the enemy based on the high strategy and courageous leadership of the Battle of Truth, Operation Bunyan-um-Marsoos


        Additional Information:

        The rank of Field Marshal is the highest rank of armies built on the patron of the British Army.

        It may be noted that after his promotion, Gen Asim Munir will become the country’s second field marshal only after Field Marshal Ayub Khan.

        165

        Muslim League was founded in which year?

        Correct Answer: 1906
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The All India Muslim League was founded on 30 December 1906 in Dacca, British India (now Dhaka, Bangladesh).
          • It's founder was Nawab Khwaja Salimullah.
          • It's first President was Sir Agha Khan.
          • The headquarters of the All India Muslim League was in Lucknow.
          • The party was dissolved on 14 August 1947.

          When the Muslim League ended in 1947, it split into three parties:

          1. Muslim League in Pakistan
          2. Awami League in Bangladesh
          3. Indian Union Muslim League in India
          166

          Who was the Defence Minister of Pakistan in 2016?

          Correct Answer: Khawaja Muhammad Asif
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Khawaja Asif served as the Defense Minister of Pakistan in 2016,

          Holding the position from November 2013 to July 2017

          During Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif's third government. 

          167

          Badshahi Masjid in Lahore is also called

          Correct Answer: Emperor mosque
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Badshahi Mosque, or the 'Emperor's Mosque', is a mosque in Pakistan.

          It was built in 1673 by the Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb in Lahore, Pakistan, near the Minar-e-Pakistan.


          168

          Liaquat Ali Khan was assassinated in Rawalpindi on _______?

          Correct Answer: 16 Oct 1951
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Liaquat Ali Khan (Born: Oct 1, 1895, Karnal, India – Died: Oct 16, 1951, Rawalpindi) was Pakistan’s first Prime Minister.

          He was assassinated on Oct 16, 1951, while addressing a gathering of 100,000 at Company Bagh, Rawalpindi (now Liaquat Bagh).

          His assassin, Said Akbar, was shot dead by police immediately.

          Buried at Mazar-e-Quaid, Karachi.

          Visited the USA in 1951.

          169

          What was the magnitude of the Earthquake that shook northern Pakistan And Azad kashmir on Oct 8, 2005?

          Correct Answer: 7.6
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The earthquake of October 2005 was result of Tectonic dislocation

          The 2005 Kashmir earthquake occurred at 08:50:39 Pakistan Standard Time on 8 October in Pakistani-administered Azad Kashmir.

          Magnitude: 7.6 Mw

           More than 80,000 people perished as a result of the quake,

          while an estimated 4 million others were left homeless


          ******

          ND11-12-2022

          The Picture on the back of 1000 Rupee Pakistani Note?

          Correct Answer: Islamia College Peshawar

          The Picture on the back of 1000 Rupee Pakistani Note Islamia College Peshawar


          • The first banknotes issued by the State Bank of Pakistan were released on the 1st of October 1948, i printed by Thomas De La Rue & Company.

          • The Picture on the back of the 100 Rupee Pakistani Note Quaid-e-Azam Residency
          • The Picture on the back of the 500 Rupee Pakistani Note Badshahi Mosque

          • The Picture on the back of the 5000 Rupee Pakistani Note Faisal Mosque

          171

          The city of textiles is__________?

          Correct Answer: Faisalabad
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The city of textiles is Faisalabad

          It was formerly known as Lyallpur

          It is famous for its textile mills

          It is the third-largest city in Pakistan

          It is the second-largest city in the Province of Punjab.

          It is called the Manchester of Pakistan because it's the biggest textile city in Pakistan

          172

          The office of Council of Islamic Ideology is located in which city of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Islamabad
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The office of the Council of Islamic Ideology (CII) is located in Islamabad, Pakistan. 

          It is a constitutional body of Pakistan, responsible for giving legal advice on Islamic issues to the government and the Parliament.

          This body was founded in 1962 under the government of Ayub Khan.

          173

          On __________ President Ayub Khan was sworn in for a second term as president.

          Correct Answer: 27 October 1958
          Show explanation

          Muhammad Ayub Khan was the second President of Pakistan from 27 October 1958 – 25 March 1969

          Ayub Khan enforced a new constitution in Pakistan on 8th June 1962

          ***

          When General Ayub Khan resigned from his Presidentship?

          25th March 1969

          ***

          Ayub Khan was born on 14 May 1907 in Rehana, a village in Abbottabad District 

          Ayub Khan died in Islamabad in April 1974.

          Ayub Khan's election symbol was Red Rose

          He went on to study at Aligarh Muslim University (AMU)

          Ayub Khan was an army general who seized the presidency from Iskander Mirza in a coup in 1958

          177

          Total number of seats in National Assembly of Pakistan are _______?

          Correct Answer: 336
          Show explanation
          Explanation


          The National Assembly of the parliament consists of 336 members old was 342 reducing KPK from 61 to 55.

          It become 342 after 26th amendment

          National Assembly is the lower  house of parliament 

          Senate is the Upper house of parliament 


          NamePunjabSindhKPKBaluchistanICTTotal
          General1416145163266
          Women3214104-60
          Non-Muslims-----10
          Total1737555203336

          According to Article 51 (1) and 3(a) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan

          Article 51. (1) There shall be [three hundred and thirty-six] seats for members in the National Assembly, including seats reserved for women and non-Muslims.

          Reference Click

          Qaumi Assembly is the lower house of the parliament.

          The National Assembly has 336 seats,

          266 of which are directly elected,

          60 are reserved for women 

          10 for religious minorities .




          178

          Pakistan is _______ hours ahead of GMT.

          Correct Answer: 5
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Pakistan Standard Time (PKT) is 5 hours ahead of Greenwich Mean Time, which means PKT is GMT+5.


          179

          Who started Jihad Movement?

          Correct Answer: Syed Ahmad Shaheed
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Syed Ahmad Shaheed (1786-1831) was a Muslim leader and reformer who founded the Jihad movement in India.

          He led a rebellion against the Sikh Empire in the early 19th century.

          The Jihad movement, also known as the Tariqah-i-Muhammadiyah.

          It aimed to reform Islamic practices, promote religious purification, and resist British colonial rule.

          Taxila is famous for ______?

          Correct Answer: Buddhism
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The old name of Taxila was Takhshasila.

          Taxila was designated a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1980.

          Taxila's Province in Punjab.

          Taxila's Division is Rawalpindi Division.

          Taxila's District is Rawalpindi District.

          Taxila's Tehsil is Taxila Tehsil.

          Old Taxila was the capital of Ghandhara.

          Old Taxila was an important city in ancient India.

          Taxila is one of Punjab's popular tourist spots.

          186

          What is the percentage of poverty in Pakistan in 2025 (according to the updated international line)?

          Correct Answer: 44.7%
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The percentage of poverty in Pakistan in 2025, according to the updated international line, is 44.7%.

          The World Bank has revised its poverty thresholds, leading to a significant increase in the reported poverty rate in Pakistan.

          187

          Which aircraft/jet of Pakistan is claimed to have destroyed Indian Rafales?

          Correct Answer: J-10
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          India launched military attack on Pakistan on 7 May 2025

          Pakistani military brought down five Indian jets in retaliation.

          India targeted Kotli, Bahawalpur, Muridke, Bagh and Muzaffarabad.

          India termed this aggression as “Operation Sindoor”.

          As per Pakistan, 31 civilians killed and 46 injured.

           

          Additional Information:

          The April 22, 2025 attack in Pahalgam killed 26 tourists.

          India, without investigation or evidence, blamed Pakistan for this.

          188

          The book “Pakistan – A Dream Gone Sour” is written by ______?

          Correct Answer: Roedad Khan
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The book reflects on Pakistan’s political and administrative challenges after independence.

          189

          Who composed the lyrics of the National Anthem of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Ahmed G. Chagla
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          About National Anthem of Pakistan:

          • Written by: Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952
          • Music by: Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949
          • Adopted on: August 13, 1954
          • First recorded by: Ahmed Rushdi in 1954
          • Re-recorded after 68 years
          Key Facts:
          • 15 lines in the National Anthem of Pakistan
          • 48 words in the National Anthem of Pakistan
          • First played in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran
          • "Jan-i-Istaqbal" means Symbol of ambitions
          • First played on national radio on August 14, 1947
          • Duration of the National Anthem of Pakistan: 80 seconds (1 minute 20 seconds)
          • Pakistan's National Anthem is written in: Persian

          When the Muslim League joined the interim government?

          Correct Answer: 26 October 1946

          The Interim Government was originally headed by the Viceroy of India

          On 26 October 1946, Muslim League entered the Interim Government

          191

          How many towns in Pakistan at the time of independence more than 100000 population?

          Correct Answer: 8
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          At the time of independence in 1947, only 8 towns in Pakistan had a population exceeding 100,000.

          These included

          1. Karachi,
          2. Lahore,
          3. Dacca,
          4. Peshawar,
          5. Rawalpindi,
          6. Hyderabad,
          7. Multan, and
          8. Sialkot.
          192

          The 1991 Industrial policies of Pakistan were related to _____?

          Correct Answer: Privatization of institutes
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          In November 1990, the government that came to power declared privatization as its primary economic policy objective.

          A major privatization program was launched on January 22, 1991.

          The government's privatization agenda covered various fields, including industries, banks, development finance institutions, and infrastructure facilities.

          The Privatisation Commission was established on January 22, 1991, to execute the disinvestment program.

          193

          In which year was the Gwadar Port Authority established?

          Correct Answer: 2002
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in the Balochistan province of Pakistan

          The Gwadar Port was Opened: in 2002;

          Pakistan purchased Gwadar port from Oman on 8th September 1958.

          Gwadar officially became part of Pakistan on 8th December 1958.

          Gwadar remained under Omani rule for 174 years from 1783 -1958 

          Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in Balochistan province.

          Opened: 2002 

          while 

          Pakistan has three big sea ports  It was purchased from Oman in 1958.

          Karachi Port,

          Muhammad Bin Qasim port

          and Gwadar port.

          194

          Gandhi called off the Civil Disobedience Movement after which incident?

          Correct Answer: Chauri Chaura Incident
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Non-Cooperation Movement started in 1920.

          The leader of the Non-cooperation Movement was Mahatma Gandhia and Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

          The non-cooperation movement was a mass movement that involved participation from the nationalists as well as the public. 

          After a series of events including the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre in 1920.

          The Non-cooperation Movement was called off by Mahatma Gandhi after the Chauri Chaura incident in February 1922.

          195

          By whose order did the massacre take place in Jallianwala Bagh?

          Correct Answer: Gen. Dyer
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on 13 April 1919

          In Jallianwala Bagh massacre killing at least 379 people and injuring over 1,200 other people.

          Jallianwala Bagh is a historic garden close to the Golden Temple complex in Amritsar, Punjab, India

          By General Reginald Dyer order did the massacre take place in Jallianwala Bagh

           It is managed by the Jallianwala Bagh National Memorial Trust and was renovated between 2019 and 2021****

          Second edition: 1993.

          Which is the oldest airline of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Orient Airways
          Show explanation

          It is founded in 1946.

          It is later merged with Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) in 1955 to become the national flag carrier.

          204

          In March 2006, who was appointed as the bowling coach for Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Waqar Younis
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          On March 08, 2006

          Pakistan appointed former captain and fast bowler Waqar Younis as their bowling coach until the 2007 World Cup.

          Waqar Younis was re-appointed as Pakistan's bowling and fielding coach for their tour of Australia in December 2009

          Waqar was appointed as the coach of the Pakistan cricket team on 3 March 2010.

          He resigned as Pakistan's Cricket Coach on 19 August 2011 citing personal reasons.

          Where the Cunningham clock tower is situated?

          Correct Answer: Peshawar
          Show explanation

          It was was built in 1900, "in commemoration of the Diamond Jubilee of Her Majesty the Queen Empress". 

          206

          K2 is also known as _____?

          Correct Answer: Godwin Austin
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          K-2 is Also Known As Godwin Austin

          K2 mountain was first measured by Godwin Austen in 1856.

          Godwin Austen was an English topographer (surveyor) 

          'K' standing for Karakoram.

          Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.

           after the world's highest peak Mount Everest.

          Mount K2 is part of the Karakorum Range located on the border of Pakistan and China.

          K2 height 8,611 meters

          It is located on the China–Pakistan border between Baltistan in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of northern Pakistan.

          207

          What is the name of Pakistan's first battle tank?

          Correct Answer: Al Khalid
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Pakistan's first indigenously developed main battle tank was the Al-Khalid

          Which was jointly developed with China in the 1990s

          And entered service in 2001.

          An upgraded version, the Al-Khalid-I, was inducted in 2020

          Rehman Baba was the poet of which language?

          Correct Answer: Pashto

          Explanation

          Rehman Baba was a legendary Pashto Sufi poet.

          Rehman Baba was a Sufi poet of Mugal era.

          Rehman Baba belonged to Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

          ***

          Rehman Baba is considered one of the most popular poets among the Pashtuns in Pakistan and Afghanistan.

          Rehman Baba was a legendary Pashto Sufi poet.

          Rehman Baba Original name: Abdur Rahmān Mohmand

          Rehman Baba was Born: in 1632, Peshawar

          Rehman Baba was Died: in 1706, Peshawar

          Rehman Baba Tomb : in Peshawar (Ring Road Hazar Khwani)

          The old name of Taxila was Takhshasila

          Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan did his Ph.D from Leaven University of ________?

          Correct Answer: Belgium
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan earned his Ph.D. from the Catholic University of Leuven in Belgium.

          He completed his doctoral studies in metallurgical engineering in 1972

           Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan is widely regarded as the "Saviour of Pakistan"

          "Mohsin-e-Pakistan" (Savior of Pakistan).

          He is colloquially known as the father of Pakistan's atomic weapons program.


          Science Advisor to the Presidential Secretariat:In office 1 January 2001 – 31 January 2004

          Born: 1 April 1936, Bhopal, Bhopal State, India

          • Hilal-i-Imtiaz (1989)
          211

          How many articles were there in the constitution of 1956?

          Correct Answer: 234 Articles
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          All important questions of  "the 1956 constitution"

          1956 constitution is promulgated on 23 March 1956

          ***

          Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 constitution

          ***

          Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution

          ***

          Constitution of 1956 · Pakistan was declared as the Islamic Republic

          ***

          In 1956 the designation of Governor General changed to President

          ****

          40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution

          **

          The Constitution of 1956 contained 234 articles divided into thirteen parts and six schedules

          **

          The constitution of 1956 remained implemented for 2 years 6 months and 14 days.

          **

          The constitution of 1956 provided a Parliamentary form of government for the country.

          **

          1956 constitution legislature was Unicameral

          **

          The Constitution of 1956 provided for a federal system with the principle of parity between East Pakistan and West Pakistan

          212

          Which is the most widely spoken language in Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Punjabi
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The percentage of Punjabi-speaking people in Pakistan is 39%.

          List of other Languages:

          Sindhi speaking in Pakistan: 15%

          Pashto speaking in Pakistan: 18%

          Urdu in speaking Pakistan: 8%

          Balochi speaking in Pakistan: 3%

          213

          Which dam is situated on Haro River?

          Correct Answer: Khanpur
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Khanpur Dam is located on the Haro River in Pakistan.
          •  Situated in the Haripur District, KPK, near Taxila.
          • Completed in 1983.
          • Opening date: 1984


          214

          The "Green Line" refers to the boundary between which two countries?

          Correct Answer: Pakistan and India
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The "Green Line" refers to the boundary between Pakistan and India.

          It specifically the ceasefire line established after the 1947-48 Indo-Pakistani War.

          215

          Which countries supported Pakistan during the Indo-Pak war of 2025?

          Correct Answer: China, Turkey & Azerbaijan
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The 2025 conflict was a brief armed conflict between India and Pakistan.

          India launched Operation Sindoor against Pakistan on 7 May 2025.

          In response, Pakistan launched its Operation Bunyan-un-Marsoos on 10 May 2025 against India.

          On 10th of May 2025, a ceasefire was announced by the US president Donald Trump.

          China, Turkey and Azerbaijan supported Pakistan. 

           

          Additional Information:

          Tensions escalated between India and Pakistan after Pahalgam terrorist attack in Indian occupied Kashmir on 22 April 2025 in which 26 civilians were killed.

           ****

          216

          First recorded poetry in urdu was by ________?

          Correct Answer: Amir khusro
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Amīr Khusrau was an Indo-Persian Sufi singer, musician, poet, and scholar

          The Full name of Amīr Khusrau : Ab'ul Hasan Yamīn ud-Dīn K͟husrau

          Khusrau is credited for the invention of the sitar.

          Amīr Khusrau lived under the Delhi Sultanate. 

          Amīr Khusrau  Born: 1253, Patiyali

          Amīr Khusrau  Died: on October 1325, in Delhi, India

          The Place of burial of Amīr Khusrau: Hazrat Nizamuddin Aulia Dargah, New Delhi, India

          whil

          Baba Farid is the first Punjabi poet.

          Allama Iqbal is the National poet of Pakistan

          Hassan Bin Sabit was the first poet of Islam

          217

          When did East Pakistan became Bangladesh?

          Correct Answer: 1971
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          On 16 December 1971, East Pakistan was liberated from Pakistan as the newly independent state of Bangladesh.

          Bangladesh is the new name of East Pakistan

          The history of East Bengal and East Pakistan from 1947 to 1971

          The territory of East Pakistan was handed in the Simla Agreement


          218

          Where is Shah Jahan's tomb located?

          Correct Answer: Agra
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Shah Jahan's tomb is located in the Taj Mahal in Agra, India.

          The Taj Mahal is a mausoleum complex that houses the tombs of both Shah Jahan and his wife, Mumtaz Mahal.

          The Mughal emperor commissioned the construction of the Taj Mahal in 1631.

          The mausoleum is made of white marble inlaid with semi-precious stones.

          It is considered a prime example of Mughal architecture.

          The Taj Mahal is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

          219

          When was the War of Independence started?

          Correct Answer: 10th May 1857
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The War of Independence started on May 10, 1857, in Meerut.
          • The rebellion was started by Indian troops in the service of the British East India Company. 
          • The rebellion was called the Sepoy Mutiny or the First War of Independence and it spread to Delhi, Agra, Kanpur and Lucknow.
          • The rebellion ended on July 8, 1859 .
          220

          Faisal Mosque is located in which city of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Islamabad
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The mosque was built by a Saudi King named King Faisal who gifted it in recognition of friendship between Pakistan and Saudi Arabia.

          Faisal Mosque of Pakistan was opened to the public in 1986 OR 1987

          **

          On 18th June 1988 the first prayer  was held in the Faisal mosque

          **

          The Faisal Mosque is the largest mosque in Pakistan.

          It is located in the national capital city of Islamabad.

          Completed in 1986,

          it was designed by Turkish architect Vedat Dalokay.

          221

          The largest province in mineral wealth of Pakistan is?

          Correct Answer: Baluchistan
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Baluchistan has the most mineral deposits among the provinces of Pakistan.

          Sindh rich in coal deposit.

          KPK rich in gems.

          Oil, gas and minerals used in nuclear energy purposes are mined by the federal government.

          222

          The hottest place in Pakistan is ____?

          Correct Answer: Turbat
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          Turbat, Balochistan is one of the hottest cities in South Asia

          Turbat has recorded temperatures as high as 53.5 degrees Celsius. 

          Turbat is the second-largest city in Balochistan after Quetta

          Turbat is the 38th largest city in Pakistan

          Turbat was the historical capital of the State of Makran

          while

          Jacobabad is another hottest cities in Pakistan

          Africa is the hottest continent on the earth

          225

          How many Number of lines in National Anthem of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: 15
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          About National Anthem

          National Anthem was written by Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952 

          the music was produced by Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949.

          It was officially adopted as Pakistan's national anthem on 13 August 1954.

          Ahmed Rushdi recorded the National Anthem of Pakistan in 1954.

          After 68 years Pakistan's National Anthem recorded

          15 lines in the National Anthem of Pakistan

          48 Words in the National Anthem of Pakistan

          The National Anthem of Pakistan was played for the first time in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran

          In the National Anthem of Pakistan, " Jan-i-Istaqbal" stands for the Symbol of ambitions.

          the first national anthem of Pakistan was played on Pakistan's national radio on 14 August 1947

          226

          Through which province of Pakistan does the Silk Road (Karakorum Highway) pass?

          Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

          The silk road connects Pakistan and China.

          The total length is approximately 1,300 kilometers, of which 887 kilometers are in Pakistan and 413 km in China.

          The silk road is considered a symbol of Pakistan-China's eternal friendship

          The highway meets the Pakistani-Chinese border at Khunjerab Pass

           It was started in 1959 and was completed and opened to the public in 1979.

          Karakorum Highway is located in the Karakoram range

          Karakorum highway in Pakistan starts from Hassan Abdal

          Karakorum highway is also called N-35

          ****

          227

          The cabinet mission arrived in India in _____?

          Correct Answer: 1946
          Show explanation
          Explanation

           Cabinet Mission broadcast its plan from New Delhi on May 16, 1946.

          They arrived in India on March 24, 1946, sent by the British government.

          The Mission was headed by Lord Pethick Lawrence.

          The mission had three British cabinet members:

          Pethick Lawrence

          Stafford Cripps

          A.V. Alexander

          The Cabinet Mission aimed to discuss the transfer of power from British to Indian leadership.

          When did Muslims demand a separate homeland?

          Correct Answer: 1940 – Lahore (Pakistan) Resolution
          Show explanation

          Known as the Pakistan Resolution, presented by A.K. Fazlul Haq and supported by Quaid-e-Azam.

          Correct Answer: Lahore Resolution

          This session of the All India Muslim League passed the Lahore Resolution, demanding a separate homeland for Muslims.

          When was the province of NWFP set up?

          232

          From which European university Allama Iqbal got his PhD Degree?

          Correct Answer: Ludwig Maximilian University
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          In 1907, Iqbal moved to Germany to pursue his doctoral studies, and earned a Doctor of Philosophy degree from the Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich in 1908. Working under the guidance of Friedrich Hommel, Iqbal's doctoral thesis was entitled The Development of Metaphysics in Persia.

          233

          What was the main reason for the end of the Khilafat Movement?

          Correct Answer: Jallianwala Bagh Tragedy
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Jallianwala Bagh massacre (1919) deeply shook Indian Muslims and weakened the Khilafat cause.

          Along with the abolition of the Caliphate by Mustafa Kemal (1924), it marked the end of the movement.

          234

          When was Khilafat Day observed?

          Correct Answer: 27th October 1919
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          Khilafat Day was observed on 27th October 1919.
          It was organized by the All India Khilafat Committee.
          Purpose: To protest against British policies regarding the Ottoman Caliphate after WWI.
          It became a turning point in Muslim political awakening in India.

          • Indian Judicial Branch

          Under the Doctrine of Lapse, which state was annexed?

          Correct Answer: Awadh (Oudh)

          It was justified on grounds of "misgovernance," not heirlessness.

          Correct Answer: Karnal (India)

          239

          Ayub Khan was born in _____?

          Correct Answer: Abbottabad
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Ayub Khan – Key Facts

          • Full Name: Muhammad Ayub Khan
          • Born: 14 May 1907, Rehana (Abbottabad District)
          • Died: April 1974, Islamabad
          • Education: Aligarh Muslim University (AMU)
          • Military Role: Army General, seized power from Iskander Mirza in 1958
          • Presidency: 27 October 1958 – 25 March 1969
          • Resigned: 25 March 1969
          • Constitution Enforced: 8 June 1962
          • Election Symbol: Red Rose

          Presidents of Pakistan:

          1. Iskander Mirza (23 March 1956 – 27 October 1958)
          2. Ayub Khan (27 October 1958 – 25 March 1969)
          3. Yahya Khan (25 March 1969 – 20 December 1971)
          4. Zulfikar Ali Bhutto (20 December 1971 – 14 August 1973)
          5. Fazal Ilahi Chaudhry (14 August 1973 – 16 September 1978)
          6. Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq (16 September 1978 – 17 August 1988)
          7. Ghulam Ishaq Khan (17 August 1988 – 18 July 1993)
          8. Farooq Leghari (14 November 1993 – 2 December 1997)
          9. Muhammad Rafiq Tarar (1 January 1998 – 20 June 2001)
          10. Pervez Musharraf (20 June 2001 – 18 August 2008)
          11. Asif Ali Zardari (9 September 2008 – 9 September 2013)
          12. Mamnoon Hussain (9 September 2013 – 9 September 2018)
          13. Arif Alvi (9 September 2018 – 10 March 2024)
          14. Asif Ali Zardari (10 March 2024 – Present)
          240

          Yahya Khan dismissed how many government officers?

          Correct Answer: 303
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          General Yahya Khan dismissed 303 senior civil servants in 1969.

          This was the largest purge of bureaucrats in Pakistan’s history.

          Who started NCC (National Cadet Corps) in Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah inaugurated the National Cadet Corps (NCC) in Pakistan to promote discipline and military training among youth.

          It was aimed at preparing students for national defense and instilling leadership qualities.

          Nizam-e-Islam party was founded by _____?

          Correct Answer: Chaudhary Muhammad Ali
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Nizam-e-Islam Party was founded by Chaudhary Muhammad Ali in 1952.

          He formed it to promote Islamic principles in politics after leaving the Muslim League.

          243

          How many members were there in the First Constituent Assembly of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: 69
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          A Gazette of India notification (26 July 1947) shaped the First Constituent Assembly with 69 members.

          Later, the membership was increased to 79, including one female member (Begum Shaista Suhrawardy Ikramullah).

          The capital of Sindh is ______?

          Correct Answer: Karachi

          2. Punjab: Lahore
          3. Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: Peshawar
          4. Balochistan: Quetta

          6. Azad Jammu and Kashmir (region): Muzaffarabad
          245

          Which language was declared as National in the 1973 constitution of Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Urdu
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The 1973 Constitution recognizes Urdu as Pakistan's only national language.

          The 1973 Constitution contained:

          •  280 articles
          •  07 schedules with Objective Resolution.
          • 12 chapters 

          Effective date: 14 August 1973

          Drafted by the Government of Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto

          The Constitution declared Pakistan an Islamic Republic and Islam as the state religion.

          Confusion

          Urdu is the national language of Pakistan since 14 August 1947.

          In 1954 Urdu and Bengali were declared the national languages of Pakistan.

           

           

          The 1973 Constitution recognizes Urdu as Pakistan’s only national language. 

          ****

           

          Urdu declared as official language In the subcontinent in 1932

          ND5-5-2023

          Six Points of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman was presented in which year?

          Correct Answer: 1966
          Show explanation

          Sheikh Mujibur Rahman announced the six points in Lahore.

          247

          Who assassinated Liaquat Ali Khan?

          Correct Answer: Syed Akbar
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Liaquat Ali Khan (Born: Oct 1, 1895, Karnal, India – Died: Oct 16, 1951, Rawalpindi) was Pakistan’s first Prime Minister.

          He was assassinated on Oct 16, 1951, while addressing a gathering of 100,000 at Company Bagh, Rawalpindi (now Liaquat Bagh).

          His assassin, Said Akbar, was shot dead by police immediately.

          Buried at Mazar-e-Quaid, Karachi.

          Visited the USA in 1951.

          When did Sir Radcliffe arrive in the subcontinent?

          Correct Answer: 8th July 1947
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Sir Cyril Radcliffe arrived in India on July 8, 1947.


          As chairman of the two Boundary Commissions for Punjab and Bengal


          He was given five weeks to define the border between India and Pakistan


          Upon meeting with Mountbatten, Radcliffe traveled to Lahore

            Correct Answer: 12 September 1965
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • On 12 September 1965 during Indo-Pak War he embraced Shahadat while fighting bravely at Burki Lahore
            • In 1950, he joined the Pakistan Army and was commissioned in the Punjab Regiment.
            • Maj Aziz Bhatti Shaheed was born in Hong Kong in 1928 and moved to Pakistan before independence in 1947, settling in Ladiyan village of district Gujrat.

            256

            Pakistan shares it's longest border with the country _____?

            Correct Answer: India
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            Pakistan's longest border is shared with India.
            The border is approximately 2,912 kilometers long. 
            It's known as the International Border (IB)
            The boundaries between India and Pakistan include the 24th Parallel, Line of Control (LoC), and the Working Boundary.
            24th parallel line is also known as Sir Creek.
            The Radcliffe Line between India and Pakistan that came into effect on August 17, 1947.
            The line was named after Sir Cyril Radcliffe.

              • He was the second Muslim scientist to get a Nobel Prize.
              • First Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize
              • Born in Jhang, Punjab, Jan. 29, 1926
              • Died: Nov. 21, 1996
              • He died in 1996.
              • Received the Nobel Prize for combining two forces: the electromagnetic and weak forces
              258

              What is the largest artificial lake in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Keenjhar Lake
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Keenjhar Lake is also known as Kalri Lake.

              It is the largest artificial lake in Pakistan, located in Thatta District, Sindh.

              It was formed by building a bund (dam) on the eastern side, uniting two lakes: Sonehri and Keenjhar.

              259

              National Vegetable of Pakistan is ________?

              Correct Answer: Lady Finger
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Bhindi is also known as lady's finger due to its finger-like shape.

              Deodar is the National tree of Pakistan

              Deodar is also known as  Himalayan Cedar (Cedrus Deodara)

              Its name is derived from 'Sanskrit' and means "Wood of the God".

              It is amongst the tallest trees in the country,

              Its heights of around 40-50 meters



              .****

               

              Additional information:

              The national bird of Pakistan Chukar

              The national animal of Pakistan markhor
              The national fruit of Pakistan               Mango
              The national dish of Pakistan               nihari
              The national flower of Pakistan               jasmine


              260

              In which province of Pakistan is the most natural gas produced?

              Correct Answer: Sindh
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sindh is the leading producer of natural gas in Pakistan.

              Major gas fields include Qadirpur, Sui Southern extensions, and Mari.

              261

              What symbolizes the white crescent (colour) in the center of Pakistan's national flag?

              Correct Answer: Peace and minorities
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              White color represents – peace and honesty & symbolizing the role of religious minorities

              Green color represents – hope, joy, and love, and in many cultures has a sacred significance as in Pakistan where the color green is associated with Islam.

              The crescent in the Pakistani Flag represents progress

              Pakistan flag's green and white color explains the Muslim majority in Pakistan and various religious minorities.

              The Dark green field in the flag of Pakistan represents Muslim Majority

              The star in the Pakistani flag represents light and knowledge

              The white strip in the flag of Pakistan represents Minority

              262

              Under which constitution of Pakistan Islamabad was confirmed as the capital of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: 1973
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Article 1(2)(b) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 declared the status of Islamabad Capital 

              Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967.

              Ayoub Khan made Islamabad the capital of Pakistan

              The area before its declaration as the capital of Pakistan was known as Raj Shahi.

              Islamabad is the country's ninth-most populous city, with a population of over 1.2 million people

              The name Islamabad means City of Islam.

              Islamabad Constructed 1960

              263

              At the time of creation Pakistan, how many Radio Stations were there?

              Correct Answer: Three
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Three radio stations existed at the time of Pakistan's independence on 14 August 1947:

              1. Peshawar (established 1935)
              2. Lahore (established 1937)
              3. Dhaka (established 1939)

              Islamabad houses the largest radio station in Pakistan.

              It is operated by the Pakistan Broadcasting Corporation (PBC).

              It is known as Radio Pakistan, the country's largest state media network.

              264

              In which language did Allama Iqbal write Tasveer-e-Dard?

              Correct Answer: Urdu
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Tasveer-e-Dard is one of Allama Iqbal’s Urdu poems.

              It reflects his deep feelings for the condition of Muslims.

              265

              Who was the Prime Minister of Pakistan during the enforcement of first constitution in 1956?

              Correct Answer: Choudhry Mohammad Ali
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Iskander Ali Mirza was the first President of Pakistan after adopting the 1956 Constitution.

              The Constitution was adopted on March 23, 1956, with 234 articles, 13 parts, and 6 schedules.


              Additional information:

              It was Pakistan's first constitution, enforced on March 23, 1956.

              After 9 years Pakistan got its first constitution.

              Choudhry Mohammad Ali was the Prime Minister during its enforcement.


              History (Iskander Ali Mirza)

              1st President of Pakistan from 23 March 1956 – 27 October 1958

              4th Governor-General of Pakistan from 7 August 1955 – 23 March 1956

              4th Minister of Interior from 24 October 1954 – 7 August 1955

              Minister of States and Frontier Regions from 24 October 1954 – 7 August 1955

              Governor of East Bengal from 29 May 1954 – 23 October 1954

              Secretary of Defence from 23 October 1947 – 6 May 1954

              Vice-President of the Republican Party from 1955–1958

              Minister of Defence from 16 October 1951 – 17 October 1951






              266

              پاکستان ریلوے کا پرانا نام کیا تھا؟

              Correct Answer: نارتھ ویسٹرن ریلوے
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              تقسیم سے پہلے پاکستان ریلوے کو نارتھ ویسٹرن ریلوے (این وی آر) کہا جاتا تھا۔
              سن 1947ء کے بعد اس کا نام بدل کر پاکستان ریلوے رکھا گیا۔
              267

              Simla deputation met with which Viceroy?

              Correct Answer: لارڈ منٹو
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              شملہ وفد 1906

              1. شملہ وفد یکم اکتوبر 1906 کو وائسرائے ہند سے ملا.

              2. شملہ وفد کا صدر سر آغا خان تھا.

              3. اس میں 35 لوگ / ارکان شامل تھے.

              4. سب سے پہلے شملہ وفد ایم اے علی گڑھ کالج کے پرنسپل سے ملا.

              5. پر. نسپل نے سیکرٹری وائسرائے کرنل ڈونلپ سے یہ میٹنگ طے کر دیا، ان ہی کےذریعے وفد وائسرائے لارڈ منٹو سے ملا.

              6. چونکہ مسلمانوں کا یہ وفد وائسرائے ہند سے شملہ کے مقام پر ملا، اس وجہ سے اسکا نام شملہ وفد بن گیا.

               

              مسلمانوں کے خواہشات :

              1. صوبائی اور قومی اسمبلیوں میں مسلمانوں کا الگ نمایندہ ہونا چاہیے.

              2. کو نسل میں مسلمانوں کے سیٹ زیادہ ہونا چاہیے.

              اس پر سیکٹری آف دی اسٹیٹ جان مارلے خوش نہیں تھا.

              لیکن وائسرائے لارڈ منٹو مسلمانوں کے ساتھ خوش تھا.

              اس وقت برطانیہ میں لیبر پارٹی بر سر اقتدار تھی جو 1905 میں آئی تھی.

               

              268

              When Shah Wali Ullah died?

              Correct Answer: 1762
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              Real name of Shah Waliullah: Qutubuddin Ahmad Syed

              51 books were written by shah wali ullah

              Shah Waliullah Dehlawi was Born in 21 February 1703 in Phulat, Mughal Empire

              Shah Waliullah Dehlawi Died in 20 August 1762 (aged 59) Dehli, Mughal Empire

              Shah Waliullah first time in history translated the Holy Quran into the Persian Language

              Shah Wali Ullah was born in the reign of Aurangzeb

              ****

              nd9-2-2023



              269

              Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was died in _____?

              Correct Answer: 1898
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sir Syed Ahmad Khan died on March 27, 1898.

              He is buried right along with the Sir Syed Masjid inside Aligarh University, Aligarh UP India.

              Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was born in 1817 in Delhi

              Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was an Islamic reformer and philosopher

               Mir Muttaql was the father of Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.

              Aziz-un-Nisa was the mother of sir syed Ahmed Khan's


              270

              When did Sir Syed establish Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College?

              Correct Answer: 1875
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Sir Syed Ahmad Khan established the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College
              • Foundation Date: May 24, 1875 (as a school).
              • Location: Aligarh, Uttar Pradesh.
              • Became a college two years later (1877)
              • Known initially as Madarsatul Uloom
              • Inspired by the Cambridge education system,
              • Began operations on Queen Victoria's 56th birthday
              • Intermediate classes were introduced in 1878.
              • B.A. degree classes in 1881
              • Aligarh Muslim University in 1920


              Last updated question by NA 22-April-2026

              271

              Which is the largest newspaper in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Jang
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              Daily Jang is the largest circulated Urdu newspaper in Pakistan.
              It has a wide readership across major cities like Karachi, Lahore, and Islamabad.
              272

              Where the Pakistan’s first radio station was setup?

              Correct Answer: Karachi
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan’s first radio station was set up in Karachi.

               

              There are the following three radio stations existed at the time of independence of Pakistan on 14 August 1947.

              1.      Peshawar Radio Station established in 1935

              2.      Lahore radio station established in 1937

              3.      Dhaka radio station established in 1939

              ******


              273

              When Quaid-e-Azam addressed the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: 11 August 1947
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Quaid e Azam M A Jinnah, the first president of Constituent Assembly of Pakistan addressed the first constituent Assembly on 11 August 1947.
              • This speech is also known as Eleventh August Speech. 
              • Quaid e Azam M A Jinnah is also the founder of Pakistan. 
              • ***
              274

              Which Pakistani police officer received the 2025 Excellence and Performance Award from the International Association of Women Police?

              Correct Answer: Aisha Butt
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Punjab Police officer SP Aisha Butt is set to receive the Excellence in Performance Award 2025 from the IAWP.

              The award honours her "distinguished service and commitment to policing,".

              SP Butt will be presented the award during the IAWP's in 2025.

              The Annual Training Conference of IAWP will be held in Glasgow, Scotland. 

               

              Additional Information:

              IAWP stands for International Association of Women Police.

               ***

              275

              Who among the following has been appointed as Brand Ambassador for Women Empowerment in 2025?

              Correct Answer: General (R) Nigar Johar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In 2025, Gen (R) Nigar Johar Khan, Pakistan's first female Surgeon General, has been appointed Brand Ambassador for Women Empowerment by the Rawalpindi Chamber of Commerce and Industry (RCCI).

              In 2021, retired Lt Gen Johar became the first-ever female general to be appointed the colonel commandant of Army Medical Corps (AMC).

              Earlier in 2020, she became Pakistan’s first female army officer to be promoted to the rank of lieutenant general.

              ****

              276

              Who has become first female Christian Deputy Commissioner of Sindh?

              Correct Answer: Sara Javed
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sara Javed has become first Christian Deputy Commissioner of Sindh.

              She was appointed Deputy Commissioner of Sanghar district in 2025.

              She is a CSS Qualifier since 2014.

              During the Covid-19 pandemic, she was recognised with a national-level award for her work as part of the District South administration in Karachi.

              ***

              277

              The official language of Pakistan is ______?

              Correct Answer: English & Urdu
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Urdu is the national language and one of the official languages.

              English is also an official language, used in government, legal, and educational contexts.

              278

              The first Martial Law was imposed in Pakistan in the which year?

              Correct Answer: 1958
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              The first martial law was imposed on 07-October-1958.

              Skandar Ali Mirza abrogates the constitution of 1956 on 07-October-1958.

              At the time of the first Martial Law:

              • Ayoub Khan was made chief of martial law after this abrogation.
              • Feroz Khan Noun was the prime minister

              ******

              There are a total of four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

              Sr#

               Martial Law Number

              Date of Martial Law

              Martial Law Implemented by

              1

              First Martial Law

              7th October 1958

              Iskandar Mirza

              2

              2nd Martial Law

              25th March 1969

              General Yahya Khan

              3

              3rd Martial Law

              25th July 1977

              General Zia ul Haq

              4

              4th Martial Law

              12th October 1999

              General Pervaiz Musharraf

               


              279

              تحریک پاکستان کے دوران "یقین، اتحاد، قربانی" کا نعرہ کس نے دیا؟

              Correct Answer: قائد اعظم
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              قائد اعظم نے تحریک پاکستان کے دوران نوجوانوں کو جوش دلانے کے لیے "یقین، اتحاد اور قربانی" کا نعرہ دیا۔

              یہ نعرہ برصغیر کے مسلمانوں کے لیے ایک رہنمائی اصول بن گیا۔

              280

              Defence day is celebrated in Pakistan on _______?

              Correct Answer: 6th September
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              Defence Day is celebrated as the sacrifices made by Pakistani soldiers in defending its borders.

              Defence Day is celebrated in Pakistan on September 6
              The event Length of Day is 1 Day

              The day was first celebrated in 1965

              Defence Day is Also called: Youm-e-Difa


              ****


              281

              Who is the current Deputy Speaker of provincial assembly of Punjab?

              Correct Answer: Zaheer Iqbal Channar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Zaheer Iqbal Channar became Deputy Speaker of Punjab Assembly on in March 2024

              He replaced Wasiq Qayyum Abbasi.

              He belongs to PML-N.

              His constituency is PP-253 Bahawalpur-IX.



              Updated & verified on 28 August 2025 

              282

              Keenjhar & Haleji lakes are located in ______ district of Sindh province.

              Correct Answer: Thatta
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hub, Haleji, Keenjhar, and Manchhar lakes are located in Sindh province.

              Hub Lake: Near Karachi, bordering Lasbela District, Balochistan.

              Keenjhar Lake and Haleji Lake: Located in Thatta District.

              Manchhar Lake: Situated in Jamshoro District.

              283

              Kunri a small town in Umerkot, is rightly known as one of the largest production centers for ____ in Asia.

              Correct Answer: Red chilies
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Kunri (Umerkot, Sindh) is famous as the “chili capital of Asia” due to its massive production of red chilies.

              It contributes around 85% of these chilies.

              It supplies a major share of Pakistan’s chili exports.

              284

              Tenure of National and Provincial Assemblies is?

              Correct Answer: 05 years
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              Tenure of National and Provincial Assemblies is 5 years.
              Founded in 1973
              It is also referred to as Aiwan-e-Zairin
              It is the lower house of the bicameral Parliament of Pakistan, with the upper house being the Senate
              Seats: 336
              Last election: 8 February 2024
              Next election: 2029
              Meeting place: National Assembly Secretariat, Islamabad

              The first session of the first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was held on 10 August 1947 at the Sindh Assembly Building in Karachi.


              285

              The most popular breed of buffalo in Sindh is ______?

              Correct Answer: Kundhi
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              Kundhi buffalo is the most popular and best-known breed in Sindh, Pakistan.
              It is valued for its high milk yield and adaptability to the Sindh climate.
              286

              Which one of the following divisions of Sindh is more famous for Sindhri mango production?

              Correct Answer: Mirpur Khas
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mirpur Khas division in Sindh is world-famous for producing the Sindhri mango variety.

              It is known as the “City of Mangoes” due to its high-quality mango production.

              287

              Like Khairpur in Sindh, Panjgur is more famous district of Baluchistan for the production of ______?

              Correct Answer: Dates
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Like Khairpur in Sindh, Panjgur in Balochistan is famous for date production.

              Panjgur is a significant date-producing region in Pakistan.

              Dates from Panjgur contribute to Pakistan’s position as a leading date-producing country in the world.

              288

              Pakistan played the Final of ICC T20 World Cup 2022 against _____?

              Correct Answer: England
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan reached the ICC T20 World Cup 2022 Final and faced England at Melbourne.

              England won the match by 5 wickets, becoming T20 champions.

              289

              In Pakistan the 1st case of Corona (Covid-19) virus was reported from Karachi on ____?

              Correct Answer: 26 February 2020
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The first confirmed case of Covid-19 in Pakistan was reported in Karachi on 26 February 2020.

              The patient had recently returned from Iran, where the outbreak had spread.

              290

              In Pakistan most of the sugar factories extract sugar from _____?

              Correct Answer: Sugarcane
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan’s climate favors sugarcane cultivation, making it the main raw material for sugar production.

              Pakistan is among the top global producers of sugarcane, with cultivation concentrated in Punjab, Sindh, and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces.

              291

              Which crop is called the Golden Fiber?

              Correct Answer: Jute
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Jute was also known as the ‘Golden Fibre’.

              It is mainly because of its colour.

              It grows well on alluvial soil

              Requires high temperature, heavy rainfall, and humid climate.

              This crop is grown in the tropical areas.

              India and Bangladesh are the leading producers of jute.

              292

              ZTBL stands for_____?

              Correct Answer: Zarai Taraqiati Bank Limited
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              ZTBL stands for Zarai Tarqiati Bank Limited

              Founded: 1961

              Headquarters: Islamabad

              293

              The city of Mangoes is called to which city of sindh?

              Correct Answer: Mirpurkhas
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • MirpurKhas is known for its mango cultivation.
                • It is also called the “City of Mangoes”.
                • It has been home to an annual mango festival Pakistan since 1955.
                294

                Rohri canal takes off/starts from _____?

                Correct Answer: Sukkur Barrage
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Rohri Canal originates from the left bank of the Indus River at the Sukkur Barrage.

                It is one of the main canals of the Sukkur Barrage system.

                It plays a vital role in providing irrigation water to vast agricultural lands in the Sindh province of Pakistan.

                295

                Which city/district of sindh is famous for Date Palms (Khajoor) production?

                Correct Answer: Khairpur
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Khairpur is a city and the capital of the Khairpur District.

                Khairpur is famous for Faiz Mahal and Kot Diji Fort.


                It is the 46th largest city in Pakistan by population.

                District: Khairpur

                Division: Sukkur

                Province: Sindh

                The district is further divided into eight sub-districts:

                1. Khairpur Tehsil,
                2. Mirwah Tehsil,
                3. Kot Diji Tehsil,
                4. Kingri Tehsil,
                5. Sobho Dero Tehsil,
                6. Gambat Tehsil,
                7. Faiz Ganj Tehsil
                8. and Nara Tehsil
                296

                When was the inauguration of the Kartarpur Corridor?

                Correct Answer: November 9, 2019
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Kartarpur Corridor was inaugurated on November 9, 2019.

                The Kartarpur Corridor is a visa-free border crossing and religious corridor.

                It connects the city of Dera Baba Nanak in India's Punjab state to the city of Narowal in Pakistan's Punjab province.

                It facilitating pilgrimage for Sikh devotees to visit the Gurdwara Darbar Sahib in Kartarpur, Pakistan.

                297

                Which province of Pakistan has highest number of polio cases as of 2025?

                Correct Answer: KPK
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK) has reported the highest number of wild poliovirus (WPV) cases in Pakistan.

                A total of 15 polio cases were reported from KPK during the first six months of 2025.

                Pakistan is one of the last two countries in the world, alongside Afghanistan, where polio remains endemic.

                ****

                298

                NADRA stands for ______?

                Correct Answer: National Database and Registration Authority
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • NADRA stands for National Database and Registration Authority
                • Motto: Empowerment through Identity
                • Under the control of the Interior Secretary of Pakistan
                • Established on 10 March 2000
                • Headquarters: Islamabad

                Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026


                ﻿

                299

                Which of these rivers pass through Azad Kashmir?

                Correct Answer: Jhelum
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Jhelum River flows through Azad Kashmir before entering Punjab, Pakistan.

                It flows specifically through the districts of Muzaffarabad and Mirpur.

                300

                Pakistan Steel Mill Karachi was established with the cooperation of ______?

                Correct Answer: Russia (USSR)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistan Steel Mill Karachi was founded with the support of Russia (the Soviet Union (USSR).

                In 1971, Pakistan and the Soviet Union government agreement agreed

                To provide techno-financial assistance for the construction of a steel Mill in karachi



                Additional information:

                Still, Mill was founded by Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto

                It was founded on 30th December, 1973 

                It is located in Karachi.

                It is the country's largest industrial undertaking

                Production Capacity: 1.1 Million Ton of Steel Expandable up to 3.0 Million Ton per annum.
                301

                Where is the French Beach located?

                Correct Answer: Karachi
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                French Beach of Karachi is located halfway between Hawkes Bay and Paradise Point, Karachi

                It is known to the locals as Haji Ismill Goth.

                It is reported that the beach got its name from a French couple, who visited the beach sometime during the 1900s,

                302

                What is the Term of National Assembly of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: 5 Years
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                There are a total of 336 seats in the National Assembly.

                Of these, 266 are filled by direct elections.

                In addition, the Pakistani Constitution reserves 10 seats for religious minorities and 

                60 seats for women

                *****

                303

                K-2 is present in which mountains range?

                Correct Answer: Karakoram
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                K-2 is Also Known As Godwin Austin

                K2 mountain was first measured by Godwin Austen in 1856.

                Godwin Austen was an English topographer (surveyor) 

                'K' standing for Karakoram.

                Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.

                 after the world's highest peak Mount Everest.

                Mount K2 is part of the Karakorum Range located on the border of Pakistan and China.

                K2 height 8,611 meters

                It is located on the China–Pakistan border between Baltistan in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of northern Pakistan.

                304

                One of the hottest places in the world is _____?

                Correct Answer: Jacobabad
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Jacobabad has recorded temperatures exceeding 52°C (126°F).

                This making it one of the hottest cities globally.

                The city faces severe heatwaves and arid conditions, impacting daily life and agriculture.

                305

                Pakistan used which missile series against India during Indo-Pak war 2025?

                Correct Answer: Fatah series
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The 2025 conflict was a brief armed conflict between India and Pakistan.

                India launched Operation Sindoor against Pakistan on 7 May 2025.

                In response, Pakistan launched its Operation Bunyan-un-Marsoos on 10 May 2025 against India.

                On 10th of May 2025, a ceasefire was announced by the US president Donald Trump.

                Pakistan used Fatah series during this war 

                 

                Additional Information:

                Tensions escalated between India and Pakistan after Pahalgam terrorist attack in Indian occupied Kashmir on 22 April 2025 in which 26 civilians were killed.

                 ****

                306

                What technology did Pakistan use to down Indian fighter jets during short war of 2025?

                Correct Answer: Chinese
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                India launched military attack on Pakistan on 7 May 2025

                Pakistani military brought down five Indian jets in retaliation by using Chinese technology

                India targeted Kotli, Bahawalpur, Muridke, Bagh and Muzaffarabad.

                India termed this aggression as “Operation Sindoor”.

                As per Pakistan, 31 civilians killed and 46 injured.

                 

                Additional Information:

                The April 22, 2025 attack in Pahalgam killed 26 tourists.

                India, without investigation or evidence, blamed Pakistan for this.

                ***** 

                307

                Durand Line separate which two countries?

                Correct Answer: Pakistan and Afghanistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The length of the common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km

                ...

                The common border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is called Durand Line

                ..

                British signed the Treaty of Durand line with Abur Rehman Khan

                The agreement was signed on November 12, 1893, in Kabul, Afghanistan.

                The agreement was signed between Sir Mortimer Durand, the Indian Foreign Secretary at the time and Amir Abdur Rahman Khan in Kabul.

                ********

                According to the Survey of Pakistan, the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km

                ND18-2-2023

                308

                Which one is the capital of Punjab?

                Correct Answer: Lahore
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Lahore is the second largest city of Pakistan.

                Lahore is the capital of Punjab province.

                309

                Which is the second largest province of Pakistan in terms of population?

                Correct Answer: Sindh
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Sindh is a province of Pakistan.

                Sindh is known as Bab-ul-Islam

                Sindh is the third-largest province of Pakistan by area 

                Around 23% population of Pakistan live in Sindh.

                Karachi is the largest city and capital of Sindh.

                Punjab is the largest

                while

                Sindh is the second-largest province of Pakistan in terms of population.

                Sindh has 30 Districts and 7 district

                The mountain range located in Sindh is Kirthar Range

                There are three Barrages in Sindh

                1. Guddu Barrage

                foundation-stone of Guddu Barrage on 2 February 1957.

                2. Kotri Barrage

                it was completed in 1955.

                3. Sukkur Barrage

                it was built during the British Raj from 1923 to 1932 and was named Lloyd Barrage




                310

                During the periods which one of the following traders first came to India?

                Correct Answer: Portuguese
                Show explanation
                Explanation
