                Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama was the first European to reach India

                Through the Atlantic Ocean at Calicut India.

                the Portuguese build their first fortress in Cochin.

                Francisco de Almeida was appointed as the first Portuguese governor in India.

                They were followed by the Dutch when they tried to enter the Indian market in the middle of the 16th century.



                311

                Pakistan issued which coin to commemorate Marke-e-Haq on 79th Independence day 2025?

                Correct Answer: Rs.75
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                On 14th August 2025, The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) issued a Rs. 75 commemorative coin to honor the bravery of Pakistan's armed forces during the Marka-e-Haq and to mark the country's Independence Day with due dignity.

                ***


                312

                In August 2025, Pakistan and which country formally established diplomatic relations?

                Correct Answer: Armenia
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistan and Armenia formally established diplomatic relations on August 31, 2025.

                This development came on the sidelines of the SCO Summit 2025.

                 

                Additional Information:

                Previously, Pakistan and Armenia did not have formal diplomatic relations.

                The relationship was characterised by a regional geopolitical rivalry, with Pakistan supporting Azerbaijan against Armenia in the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict.

                 ****

                313

                Pakistan formally recognized which country in August 2025?

                Correct Answer: Armenia
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistan and Armenia formally established diplomatic relations on August 31, 2025.

                This development came on the sidelines of the SCO Summit 2025.

                 

                Additional Information:

                Previously, Pakistan and Armenia did not have formal diplomatic relations.

                The relationship was characterised by a regional geopolitical rivalry, with Pakistan supporting Azerbaijan against Armenia in the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict.

                 ****

                314

                Which is the smallest division of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Karachi
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Largest: Chagai, Balochistan — 44,748 km2 (17,277 sq mi)

                Smallest: Nazimabad (Karachi Central), Sindh — 69 km2 (27 sq mi)

                Nazimabad District, formerly Karachi Central District,

                It is an administrative district of Karachi Division in Sindh, Pakistan, created in 1996.

                Karachi is the also smallest division of Sindh.

                Established in 1996 

                Disbanded: 14 August 2001

                Re-established: 11 July 2011

                315

                Which article of the Constitution provides that the judiciary shall be separated progressively from the Executive?

                Correct Answer: Article 175(3)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Article 175(3) of the 1973 Constitution directs that the judiciary shall be separated from the executive progressively.

                This provision ensures judicial independence in Pakistan.

                316

                Which of the following is not a fundamental right provided by the Constitution of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Promotion of social justice and eradication of social evils
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                This falls under the Principles of Policy (Directive Principles), not enforceable as a Fundamental Right.

                Fundamental Rights include fair trial, security of person, freedom of trade/profession, etc., but not this.

                317

                The fundamental right of fair trial and due process under Article 10-A of the Constitution 1973 is available at which stage of the proceedings?

                Correct Answer: All of these
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The fundamental right of fair trial and due process under Article 10-A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 is available at all stages of proceedings, including:

                • Lodging of complaint,
                • Service of summons,
                • Framing of charge and institution of suit,
                • Trial stage,
                • Appellate stage,
                • And any other stage where the rights of an individual are determined by a court or tribunal.
                318

                According to the Constitution 1973, which of the following territory is not part of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Azad Jammu and Kashmir
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Under the 1973 Constitution, AJK is not formally a province or territory of Pakistan but is administered separately with its own constitution and government.

                Gwadar, Tribal Areas (now merged with KP), and Islamabad Capital Territory are officially part of Pakistan.

                319

                Which of the following is not provided for by the 1962 Constitution of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Chapter on fundamental rights
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The 1962 Constitution (Ayub Khan’s) did not include fundamental rights; they were restored later in 1964 through an amendment.

                It mainly provided for a strong presidential system, Basic Democracies, and a central list of subjects only.

                320

                The first constitution of Pakistan was abrogated in favor of the Legal Framework Order (LFO) on ______?

                Correct Answer: 7th October 1958
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                On 7th October 1958, President Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 Constitution and imposed martial law.
                This paved the way for General Ayub Khan’s takeover under the Legal Framework Order (LFO).
                321

                Which one of the following proposals put an end to the deadlock in the drafting of the first Constitution of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Agreement on the creation of one unit
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The deadlock was mainly due to the issue of parity between East and West Pakistan.

                The One Unit scheme (1955) merged West Pakistan provinces, giving equal representation to both wings, thus ending the deadlock.

                322

                Article 8 to 28 of the Constitution deal with _____?

                Correct Answer: Fundamental rights
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Articles 8 to 28 of the 1973 Constitution guarantee Fundamental Rights of citizens.

                These include rights like equality, freedom of speech, religion, fair trial, and protection of life.

                323

                Which court has advisory jurisdiction?

                Correct Answer: Supreme Court
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Under Article 186 of the Constitution of Pakistan, the Supreme Court has advisory jurisdiction.

                The President can refer questions of law or public importance to the Supreme Court for its advisory opinion.

                324

                What did the 25th amendment to the constitution achieve?

                Correct Answer: FATA merged with KPK
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                FATA was merged with KPK in May 2018.

                FATA stands for Federally Administered Tribal Areas

                FATA was merged with KPK under 25th amendment. 

                After the merger of the Federally Administered Tribal Areas with KPK, FATA seats were abolished and there will be no election on 4 of the 8 seats. The rest of the 4 seats will also not be renewed in 2024.

                325

                The Supreme Commander of Pakistan Armed Forces is?

                Correct Answer: President
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                 The Supreme Commander of the Pakistan Armed Forces is the President.
                 Article 243 defines the Command of Armed Forces.
                The President is indirectly elected by the Electoral College for a five-year term.
                The Constitution requires the President to be a Muslim who is at least 45 years old.
                326

                What is the age of Retirement of a Judge of Supreme Court in Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: 65
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                A Judge of the Supreme Court can hold his office until the age of 65 years

                This age was decided in the 6th amendment of the Constitution.

                Additional information

                Supreme Court comprises 17 Judges 

                High Court Judge can hold his office until 62 years. 

                327

                Retirement age of High Court Judge is _____?

                Correct Answer: 62 years
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Retirement age of High Court Judge is: 62 years.

                The retirement age of Supreme Court Judge is: 65 years.

                Unless he sooner resigns or is removed from office in accordance with the Constitution.

                328

                How many writs are provided in the constitution?

                Correct Answer: Five writs
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Constitution provides five writs:

                1. Habeas Corpus
                2. Mandamus
                3. Prohibition
                4. Certiorari
                5. Quo Warranto

                These writs protect fundamental rights and ensure judicial control over authorities.

                329

                Where is the Principle Seat of Supreme Court located?

                Correct Answer: Islamabad
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Principal Seat of the Supreme Court of Pakistan is in Islamabad.

                However, registries also exist in Lahore, Karachi, Peshawar, and Quetta.

                The Supreme Court of Pakistan is the highest appellate court of the country and court of last resort.

                It is the final arbiter of the law and the Constitution. 

                All executive and judicial authorities are bound to act in aid of the Supreme Court. 

                It comprises 17 Judges.

                330

                How many Majlis-e-Shura (parliament houses) are in Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Two
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Majlis-e-Shura means:

                Majlis-e-Shura, religious body regarding caliphate.

                Shura Council, house of the Egyptian Parliament. 

                Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament) of Pakistan consists of the President and two Houses 

                ****

                Two Majlis-e-Shura (parliament houses) in Pakistan known as:

                1. the National Assembly (lower house)

                National Assembly political groups are Government (179), Opposition (150)

                Total of 342 seats in it

                2. the Senate. (upper house)

                Senate political groups are Government (67), Opposition (33)

                It has a total of 100 seats

                ***

                On 24th December 1981, under Presidential Order (P.O. 15 of 1981) a Federal Council (Majlis-e-Shoora) was constituted by the President.

                Article 50 of the Constitution provides that the two houses 

                ND13-5-2023

                331

                According to the Constitution of Pakistan, the Chief Executive and most powerful person in Pakistan is _____?

                Correct Answer: Prime Minister
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Prime Minister is the Chief Executive who heads and exercises the authority of the Government of Pakistan.

                After obtaining a vote of confidence, the Prime Minister is invited by the President to take the oath of office and form the government.

                Term 5 years.

                332

                As per Article 188 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Supreme Court shall have power, subject to the provisions of any Act of 1 Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament) and of any rules made by the Supreme Court, to _____ any judgment pronounced or any order made by it.

                Correct Answer: Review
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Article 188 grants the Supreme Court the power to review its own judgments or orders.

                This ensures correction of errors and upholds justice within the Court’s own decisions.

                333

                The Federal Shariat Court declared _____ that the Professionals the MFLO, 1961 were repugnant to Islam.

                Correct Answer: Allah Rakha Case
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Federal Shariat Court declared that the provisions of the Muslim Family Laws Ordinance (MFLO), 1961, were repugnant to the injunctions of Islam in the Allah Rakha Case.

                The specific case is reported as Allah Rakha and others Vs. Federation of Pakistan and others (PLD 2000 Federal Shariat Court 1). 

                In this judgment, the court declared Section 4 and Section 7 of the MFLO to be void as they were in conflict with Islamic injunctions.

                This was a significant ruling that extended the Federal Shariat Court's authority over matters concerning Muslim Family Law. 

                334

                Article _____ of the Constitution deals with the formation of the Supreme Judicial Council.

                Correct Answer: 209
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Article 209 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973 provides for the formation of the Supreme Judicial Council.

                The Council is responsible for inquiry into misconduct or incapacity of judges of superior courts.

                335

                Which of the following is not a fundamental provided by the Constitution of Pakistan 1973?

                Correct Answer: Protection of family
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The 1973 Constitution explicitly provides fundamental rights like right to education, freedom of association, and freedom of assembly.

                Protection of family is not listed as a fundamental right under the Constitution.

                336

                Article 184 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973 provides _____?

                Correct Answer: Original jurisdiction of the Supreme Court of Pakistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Article 184 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973, provides for the Supreme Court's original jurisdiction in two distinct scenarios. 

                It empowers the Court to act directly without lower court proceedings.

                337

                Concurrent legislative list of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973 was omitted by ______?

                Correct Answer: 18th amendment
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The 18th Amendment (2010) to the Constitution of Pakistan abolished the Concurrent Legislative List.

                Its subjects were transferred mainly to the provinces, strengthening provincial autonomy.

                338

                Article 4 of the Constitution cannot be suspended during the proclamation of emergency by the State under Article 233(1)?

                Correct Answer: True
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Article 4 of the Constitution of Pakistan (right of individuals to be dealt with in accordance with law) cannot be suspended even during an emergency under Article 233(1).

                This protects the rule of law and basic rights in all circumstances.

                339

                Who abrogated 1956 constitution?

                Correct Answer: Iskander Mirza
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                All important questions of  "the 1956 constitution"

                1956 constitution is promulgated on 23 March 1956

                ***

                Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 constitution

                ***

                Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution

                ***

                Constitution of 1956 · Pakistan was declared as the Islamic Republic

                ***

                In 1956 the designation of Governor General changed to President

                ****

                40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution

                ***

                The Constitution of 1956 contained 234 articles divided into thirteen parts and six schedules

                **

                The constitution of 1956 remained implemented for 2 years 6 months and 14 days.

                **

                The constitution of 1956 provided a Parliamentary form of government for the country.

                ***

                1956 constitution legislature was Unicameral

                ***

                The Constitution of 1956 provided for a federal system with the principle of parity between East Pakistan and West Pakistan


                ND27-12-2022

                Sh26-8-2023


                  340

                  Article 58(1) of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973, the President shall disclose the National Assembly if so advised by _____ of Pakistan.

                  Correct Answer: Prime Minister
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Article 58(1) of the Constitution 1973 empowers the President to dissolve the National Assembly only on the advice of the Prime Minister.

                  This ensures that dissolution is exercised through democratic advice, not unilateral presidential power.

                  341

                  Mohtarma Fatima Jinnah had which of the following Occupation (Profession)?

                  Correct Answer: Dentist
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Fatima Jinnah: younger sister of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah

                  Fatima Jinnah born: 30th July 1893

                  Fatima Jinnah is referred as: Madr-e-Millat (Mother of Nation)

                  Fatima Jinnah died: 9 July 1967

                  Fatima Jinnah died at age: 71

                  Fatima Jinnah died: Karachi

                  Fatima Jinnah profession: Dental Surgeon

                  342

                  Who is only person from the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) to receive the Nishan-e-Haider ?

                  Correct Answer: Rashid Minhas
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pilot Officer Rashid Minhas is the only person from the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) to receive the Nishan-e-Haider. 

                  - Date of Martyrdom: August 20, 1971  

                  - Incident: Attempted hijacking of his aircraft by a defecting instructor pilot (Matiur Rahman)  

                  - Action: Rashid Minhas crashed the plane deliberately to prevent the hijack, sacrificing his life.  

                  - Age at Death: 20 years old (youngest recipient of Nishan-e-Haider)  

                  - Hometown: Karachi, Sindh  

                  - Legacy: National hero; PAF bases and roads named in his honor.

                  343

                  The Islamic Summit Conference was held in Lahore in _____?

                  Correct Answer: 1974
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  Pakistan hosted theSecond OIC Summit Conferencein February 1974 in Lahore.
                  An Extraordinary OIC Summit was also held in Islamabad in
                  1997 on the occasion of Pakistan's Golden Jubilee.

                  11th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference (Session of The Muslim Ummah in The 21St Century)

                  Dakar, Senegal (13-14 March 2008)

                  *******

                  10th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Putrajaya, Malaysia

                   (16-17 October 2003)

                  *******

                  9th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Doha, State of Qatar (12-13 November 2000)

                  *******

                  8th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Tehran, Islamic Republic of Iran (9-11 December 1997)

                  *******

                  7th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco

                   (13-15 December 1994)

                  *******

                  6th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Dakar, Republic of Senegal (9 - 11 December 1991)

                  *******

                  5th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Kuwait, State of Kuwait (26-29 January 1987)

                  *******

                  4th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco

                   (16-19 January 1984)

                  *******

                  3rd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Makkah Al-Mukarramah, Saudi Arabia (25-28 January 1981)

                  *******

                  2nd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Lahore,  Islamic Republic of Pakistan (22 - 24 February 1974)

                  *******

                  1st Session of The Islamic Summit Conference

                  Rabat, Kingdom of Morocco (September 1969)

                  ******

                  344

                  When First martial law was imposed in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Oct 7 1958
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                  Sr#

                   Martial Law Number

                  Date of Martial Law

                  Martial Law Implemented by

                  1

                  First Martial Law

                  7th October 1958

                  Iskandar Mirza

                  2

                  2nd Martial Law

                  25th March 1969

                  General Yahya Khan

                  3

                  3rd Martial Law

                  25th July 1977

                  General Zia ul Haq

                  4

                  4th Martial Law

                  12th October 1999

                  General Pervaiz Musharraf

                   

                  There are total four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                  345

                  Which Pakistani athlete won silver medal in South Asian Regional Badminton Championship 2025?

                  Correct Answer: Sarwat Fatima
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Sarwat Fatima won a silver medal for Pakistan in the South Asian Regional Badminton Championship.

                  She belongs to Balochistan.

                  This championship was held in Bhutan in August 2025.

                  ****

                  346

                  Pakistan became a Republic on ________?

                  Correct Answer: 23rd March 1956
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  After nine years of efforts, Pakistan successfully framed a constitution.

                  The Constituent Assembly adopted it on February 29, 1956.

                  The constitution consisted of 234 articles divided into 13 parts and 6 schedules.

                  On March 23, 1956, Pakistan was proclaimed an Islamic republic, officially renaming itself the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

                  March 23rd became Pakistan's Republic Day.

                  347

                  Arya Samaj was founded in _______?

                  Correct Answer: 1875
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Dayanand Saraswati was an Indian philosopher, social leader and founder of the Arya Samaj.

                  Arya Samaj founded in 1875.

                  348

                  Who was responsible for the introduction of English as the official language in India?

                  Correct Answer: Lord William Bentinck
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Lord William Bentinck is credited with introducing English as the official language of instruction in India.

                  It primarily through the "English Education Act of 1835" which he implemented alongside Thomas Babington Macaulay.

                  349

                  Syed Ameer Ali founded Central National Mohammed Association Calcutta in ____?

                  Correct Answer: 1877
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Founder: Syed Ameer Ali
                  • Organization: Central National Muhammedan Association
                  • Location: Calcutta
                  • Year: 1877
                  • An independent body and not a branch of All India Muslim League.
                  350

                  After the defeat of Turkey, the Khilafat system was in danger in India. A Khilafat Committee was set up for the help of Khilafat. Who was appointed the 1st Secretary of All India Khilafat Committee?

                  Correct Answer: Maulana Shaukat Ali
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In 1919, the Khilafat Committee was established to launch the Khilafat Movement.

                  Maulana Shaukat Ali was decided as the General Secretary of the Khilafat Movement

                  Mahatma Gandhi was elected as President (23rd November 1919)


                  Khilafat Committee and the congress participated in the election under Act of 1919.

                  The Muslims of the Sub continent launched the Khilafat Movement in the year 1919 until 1924


                  351

                  During Pakistan Movement, from where a secret newspaper Sada-i-Pakistan was published?

                  Correct Answer: Sindh
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  A secret newspaper named Sada-i-Pakistan was published from Sindh during the Pakistan Movement.

                  It was one of several publications, both overt and clandestine, that helped mobilize support for the Muslim League and the creation of Pakistan.

                  352

                  When Quid-e-Azam resigned from the Imperial legislative Council?

                  Correct Answer: 1919
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Quaid-e-Azam resigned from the Imperial Legislative Council as a protest against Rowlatt Act. 

                  The Chaotic and Revolutionary Crimes Act of 1919, commonly known as the Rowlatt Act,

                  It was the act of the Imperial Legislative Council on March 18, 1919, in Delhi.

                  353

                  In the memory of his mother, Allama wrote a long poem. Name it?

                  Correct Answer: Walida Marhooma Ki Yaad Mein
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The poem that Allama Iqbal wrote in memory of his mother is titled Walida Marhooma Ki Yaad Mein.

                  This translates to "In the Memory of the Late Mother."

                  The poem is part of his larger collection of Urdu poetry, Bang-e-Dara, which was published in 1924.

                  Iqbal's mother, Imam Bibi, passed away on November 9, 1914, while he was studying abroad.

                  354

                  Allama Iqbal received the title of Sir in?

                  Correct Answer: 1922
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Allama Iqbal received the title of Sir in 1922 in Lahore.
                  • He was knighted by King George V for his contributions to literature.

                  Note: According to SPSC, 1923 is the correct answer.

                  355

                  In which year, Muslim Students Federation (MSF) Women Wing was established in Delhi?

                  Correct Answer: 1941
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The All-India Muslim Students Federation (AIMSF) was organized in February 1941 in Delhi under the guidance of Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

                  His sister, Fatima Jinnah, was instrumental in its formation and became an important part of the Pakistan Movement.

                  This came after the Muslim League had already established a Muslim Women's Sub-committee in 1938.

                  356

                  Indus Water Treaty disallowed Pakistan to use water of one of the following rivers?

                  Correct Answer: Sutlej
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Indus Waters Treaty disallowed Pakistan from using the water of the Sutlej river.

                  The treaty, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, divided the six rivers of the Indus Basin between India and Pakistan. 

                  Eastern Rivers (allocated to India): Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej

                  Western Rivers (allocated to Pakistan): Indus, Jhelum, and Chenab

                  357

                  Abbottabad is named after _____?

                  Correct Answer: James Abbott
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Abbottabad is named after James Abbott.

                  He is a British military officer and the first Deputy Commissioner of the Hazara region.

                  He founded the town and military cantonment in January 1853. 

                  358

                  How many species of the birds have been identified in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: 786
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan is home to 786 identified bird species, reflecting its diverse ecosystems.

                  These range from migratory species to rare and endemic birds across wetlands, forests, and mountains.

                  359

                  Mari is famous for _____?

                  Correct Answer: Gas field
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Mari is famous for a Gas field.

                  It is located in the Ghotki District of Sindh province.

                  The Mari Gas Field is one of Pakistan's largest gas reservoirs.

                  It plays a significant role in the country's energy sector.

                  360

                  When did PTV start its colour transmission?

                  Correct Answer: December 20, 1976
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  PTV started its color transmission in 1976

                  It was founded on 26 November 1964.

                  It is the first official television station


                  Additional information

                  PTV started its live transmission on November 26, 1964
                  On this date, it began its first transmission broadcasts from Lahore, followed by Dhaka.
                  Ayub Khan inaugurated this first official transmission broadcasts 
                  A third center was established in Rawalpindi-Islamabad in 1965 

                  The fourth center was established in Karachi in 1966.

                  Aslam Azhar is widely considered to be the "father of Pakistan Television"

                  361

                  Who became the Governor General of Pakistan after the ailment of Ghulam Muhammad?

                  Correct Answer: Iskander Mirza
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  After Ghulam Muhammad was removed from his post due to illness, Iskander Mirza became the Governor-General of Pakistan.

                  Mirza took office in August 1955, and held the position until March 1956.

                  When Pakistan's first constitution was adopted in 1956, the role of Governor-General was replaced by the President, and Mirza became the first President of Pakistan. 

                  362

                  Toot oil and natural gas field is located in _____?

                  Correct Answer: Punjab
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  It is one of the oldest oil-producing regions in Pakistan, with commercial production starting in 1967.
                  The field is located approximately 135 km southwest of Islamabad, in the Attock District.
                  It was originally discovered in the early 1960s (1961).
                  363

                  Abdul Qadeer Khan Nuclear Research Laboratories was set up in Kahuta in the year ______?

                  Correct Answer: 1976
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Dr. A. Q. Khan Research Laboratories (KRL) was established in Kahuta, Pakistan on July 31, 1976.

                  The KRL was founded by Pakistani nuclear physicist and metallurgical engineer Abdul Qadeer Khan.

                  He is known as the father of Pakistan's atomic bomb.

                  The lab was created in response to India's first nuclear bomb test in 1974.

                  Khan was the lab's senior scientist and chairman until he retired in 2001. 

                  364

                  What is the length of M2 (Islamabad - Lahore ) Motorway?

                  Correct Answer: 375km
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The M-2 Motorway or the Lahore–Islamabad Motorway.

                  Length: 375 km

                  Constructed: 1992

                  North end: Kashmir Highway Interchange, Islamabad

                  South end: Thokar Niaz Baig, Lahore

                  ***

                  The M8 Motorway is the longest one in Pakistan

                  ****

                  The Motorway M5 (Multan-Sukkur section)

                   M-3 (Lahore-Abdul Hakeem)

                  365

                  What is the total length of Gwadar Motorway?

                  Correct Answer: 892 km
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The M8 Motorway is the longest one in Pakistan

                  M8 is the 892KM long from Ratodero to Gwadar.

                  The Motorway is partially operational and the remaining road is currently under construction

                  The M8 will have 4-lanes

                  **History**

                  The M8 is being constructed by Pakistan's National Highway Authority. 

                  Construction of the M8 commenced on 15 October 2004.

                  366

                  The total Track’s length of Railways in Pakistan is _____?

                  Correct Answer: 11,881 kilometres (7,383 mi)
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan Railways was founded in 1861.

                  The first railway line in Pakistan was laid between Karachi-Kotri.

                  Pakistan Railways headquartered in Lahore.

                  Pakistan Railways' total length is 7,791 KM of track across Pakistan.


                  Must Read it

                  The Current Federal Minister of Railways of Pakistan is?

                  Who is the Current Inspector General of Pakistan Railways Police?

                  367

                  What is the total length of the first railway track?

                  Correct Answer: 169 Km
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The first railway track in the subcontinent was 169 km long, from Karachi to Kotri, opened on May 13, 1861.

                  This inauguration marked the foundation of the railway system in British India.

                  368

                  What is the percentage of Punjabi-speaking people in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: 39%
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The percentage of Punjabi-speaking people in Pakistan is 39%.

                  List of other Languages:

                  Sindhi speaking in Pakistan: 15%

                  Pashto speaking in Pakistan: 18%

                  Urdu in speaking Pakistan: 8%

                  Balochi speaking in Pakistan: 3%

                  369

                  After winning the Presidential election, Ayub Khan started a strategic war with India in _____?

                  Correct Answer: 1965
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 was the second conflict between the two countries over the disputed state of Jammu and Kashmir.

                  He initiated the war with Operation Gibraltar, an infiltration of Pakistani-trained fighters into Jammu and Kashmir on August 5, 1965.

                  Ayub Khan had defeated his opponent, Fatima Jinnah, in the presidential election that took place on January 2, 1965, just months before the war began. 

                  370

                  What sector has the largest labour force in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Agriculture
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Agriculture is the largest sector in terms of employment in Pakistan.

                  Around 40% of the country's labor force engaged in agricultural activities.

                  This making it the backbone of Pakistan's economy.

                  371

                  When did the first census held in the Subcontinent?

                  Correct Answer: 1881
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The first complete census was taken in the sub-continent in 1881.

                  Additional Information:

                  There have been only 7 nationwide censuses.

                  1. The First Census was held in Pakistan in 1951.
                  2. The Second Census was held in Pakistan in 1961.
                  3. The Third Census was held in Pakistan in 1972.
                  4. The Fourth Census was held in Pakistan in 1981.
                  5. The Fifth Census was held in Pakistan in 1998.
                  6. The Sixth Census was held in Pakistan in 2017.
                  7. The Seventh Census was held in Pakistan in 2023.

                  Census has been undertaken every 10 years.

                  It was beginning in 1872 under British viceroy.

                  372

                  Census in Pakistan held after how many years?

                  Correct Answer: 10 years
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  1. First 1951
                  2. Second 1961
                  3. Third 1972
                  4. Forth 1981
                  5. Fifth1998 
                  6. Sixth 2017
                  7. Seventh 2023

                  Pakistan's last completed census took place in 2023.

                  373

                  The most urbanized province of Pakistan is _____?

                  Correct Answer: Sindh
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Sindh province is the most urbanized a the provinces in Pakistan as per the res 52.02 percent of its population areas.

                  374

                  Rigging in the election was first initiated by the government of ______?

                  Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Ayub Khan’s era (1958–1969) is considered the starting point of large-scale, systematic election rigging in Pakistan.

                  His Basic Democracies system and the 1965 presidential election against Fatima Jinnah are often cited as the first major examples of state-controlled manipulation.

                  375

                  Constitution of 1962 introduced one of the following in the country?

                  Correct Answer: Indirect Democracy
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  The 1962 Constitution under Ayub Khan introduced a Basic Democracies system, where people elected local representatives who indirectly elected the President and assemblies.
                  The Constitution of 1962 envisaged a Federal State with a Presidential form of government,

                  with the National Assembly at the center and the Provincial Assemblies in the Provinces
                  ***
                  The Constitution of 1962 was promulgated by President Ayub Khan
                  on 1 March 1962 and finally came into effect on 8 June 1962.

                  Urdu and Bengali Was The Official Language 

                  Ayoub Khan was made chief of martial law after this abrogation

                  Ayoub Khan enforced the new constitution on 8th June 1962

                  Yahiya Khan abrogated the 1962 constitution and became CMLA
                  376

                  In Presidential election of 1965, Ayub Khan got which Election symbol?

                  Correct Answer: Rose
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan's second Presidential Elections were held on 2 January 1965.

                  There were four candidates: Ayub Khan, Fatima Jinnah and two obscure people with no party affiliation. 

                  377

                  The major segment of “Saraiki” speaking population lives in _____?

                  Correct Answer: Punjab
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                • The Saraiki-speaking population is primarily concentrated in the southern regions of Punjab, including Multan, Bahawalpur, and Dera Ghazi Khan.
                • It is one of the major regional languages spoken in this province.
                  • 378

                  Who was the captain of the Pak Cricket team in the 1992 World Cup?

                  Correct Answer: Imran Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Imran Khan was the captain of the Pak Cricket team in the 1992 World Cup.

                  About men’s Cricket World Cup 1992

                  Edition: Fifth

                  Format: One Day International (ODI)

                  Venue: Australia & New Zealand

                  Final held: 25 March 1992

                  Final Venue: Melbourne

                  Winner: Pakistan (First title)

                  Runners-up: England

                  Participating teams: 09

                  Total Matches: 39

                  Most wickets taken by: Wasim Akram (18 wickets)

                  **** 

                  379

                  Who is known as the Saint of the Gutters?

                  Correct Answer: Mother Teresa
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Mother Teresa was called the “Saint of the Gutters” for her selfless work among the poor, sick, and dying in Kolkata, India.

                  Mary Teresa Bojaxhiu MC better was an Albanian-Indian 

                  Additional information 

                  Mother Teresa was a public nurse 

                  For 45 years she ministered to the poor, sick, orphaned, and dying.

                  She was the founder of the Missionaries of Charity (located in Kolkata)

                  Mother Teresa receives more than 700 prizes for her charity work 


                  She was born in Skopje

                  She Died on September 5, 1997  in Kolkata, India

                  She died at the of age 87 years

                  Place of burial: The Mother House Of The Missionaries Of Charity

                  380

                  The reaction of Indian National Congress and All India Muslim League towards the Cripps Mission proposals was _____?

                  Correct Answer: Both Rejected
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Muslim League rejected the proposals because it had given the greatest importance and priority to the creation of one Indian Union.

                  The Indian National leaders reject the Cripps Mission by the demand for immediate independence.


                  The Cripps Mission was a failed attempt in late March 1942.

                  The Cripps Mission was sent by the British government to India.

                   To obtain Indian cooperation for the British war efforts in the 2nd World War.

                  The mission was headed by a senior minister Sir Stafford Cripps.

                  The objective of Cripps Mission: To reform the constitutional issues

                  381

                  In 1905, the partition of Bengal was made into _____?

                  Correct Answer: West and East Bengal
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Lord Curzon divided Bengal into two provinces on 16-10-1905

                  on October 16, 1905, by Lord Curzon, the British colonial administrator of India at the time

                  The partition of Bengal was the most important event during the rule of Lord Curzon

                  The province was divided into West Bengal, which had a majority of Hindus,

                  and East Bengal, which had a majority of Muslims

                  382

                  During the partition of Punjab in 1947, India was granted a land link road to Kashmir via?

                  Correct Answer: Gurdaspur
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  During the partition of Punjab in 1947, India was granted a land link road to Kashmir via Gurdaspur.

                  Gurdaspur is a city in the Indian state of Punjab, between the rivers Beas and Ravi.

                  The headquarters of Gurdaspur District

                  Gurdaspur is in the geographical center of the district, which shares a border with Pakistan.

                  Gurdaspur City was named after Mahant Guriya das ji.

                  383

                  The period from 1954 to 1962, is known as _____ Pakistan's Foreign Policy.

                  Correct Answer: Era of Alliances
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The period of 1954 to 1962 in Pakistan is known as the era of Alliances.

                  An alliance, in international relations, is a formal agreement between two or more states for mutual support in case of war.

                  ****

                  Pakistan – Era Of Alliances (1954 -1962)

                  Mutual Defense Assistance Agreement (1954)

                  Pakistan joined SEATO (South East Asian Treaty Organization) in 1954

                  Colombo Conference (1954)

                  CENTO (Central Treaty Organization) in 1955.

                   Bandung Conference 1955

                  Pakistan’s Policy towards Suez Canal Crisis (from 1956 to 1958)

                  Custody of Gwadar 1958

                  Pak-US Agreement of Cooperation 1959

                  Kashmir in UN (1953-1957)

                  THE INDUS WATER TREATY 1960
                  384

                  When NWFP become KPK?

                  Correct Answer: 19 April 2010
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The province of NWFP (North-West Frontier Province) was renamed Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on April 19, 2010.

                  It following the 18th Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan, with 90 votes in favor.

                  NWFP was established in 1901.

                  At the time of Pakistan's independence in 1947, the Indian National Congress was a significant political party in NWFP.

                  385

                  The Pirpur report of 1938 was prepared by ______?

                  Correct Answer: Raja Syed Muhammad Mehdi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pirpur Report was prepared by Raja Syed Muhammad Mehdi.

                  Pirpur Report was published in 1938.

                  To investigate Muslim grievances under Congress rule, the Muslim League formulated the “Pirpur Report”.

                  386

                  When did Quaid-e-Azam get title of Knighthood?

                  Correct Answer: 1925
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  The title of Quaid-e-Azam was given to Mohammad Ali Jinnah by Maulana Mazharuddin Shaheed in 1938.
                  In 1925, Viceroy Lord Reading awarded Muhammad Ali Jinnah the title “Knighthood” which stands for rank or dignity.
                  while
                  Syed Ahmed Khan received a knighthood in 1888 for his loyalty.
                  387

                  For a no-confidence motion to be successful, the support of a simple majority ______ of the total 342 members in the lower house of parliament is required.

                  Correct Answer: 172
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  • The National Assembly has 342 seats, 272 of which are directly elected.
                  • 60 are reserved for women
                  • and a further 10 for religious minorities.
                  388

                  General Pervez Musharraf ruled Pakistan for nearly ______ years.

                  Correct Answer: 9
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  General Pervez Musharraf seized power in October 1999 and resigned in August 2008.

                  His rule lasted for about 9 years, covering both military and presidential authority.

                  389

                  Who has power to grant pardons or commute sentences passed by any court of Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: President
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  Under Article 45 of the Constitution of Pakistan, the President has the power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites, or commute any sentence passed by any court.
                  This power applies to all types of punishments, including death sentences, across Pakistan.
                  390

                  Gandhi – Jinnah talks of 1944 failed due to ______?

                  Correct Answer: Two – Nation Theory
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Jinnah Gandhi talks were made in 1944.

                  Jinnah Gandhi talks were held in Bombay.

                  Talk failed because Gandhi simply refused to accept the Lahore Resolution as interpreted by Jinnah.

                  391

                  Raja Dahir was the ruler of ______?

                  Correct Answer: Sindh
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Raja Dahir was the last Hindu ruler of Sindh in present-day Pakistan.

                  In 711 CE his kingdom was invaded by the Umayyad Caliphate led by Muhammad bin Qasim

                  where Dahir died while defending his kingdom.

                  ******

                  راجہ داہر موجودہ پاکستان میں سندھ کے آخری ہندو حکمران تھے۔711عیسوی میں اس کی سلطنت پر اموی خلافت نے حملہ کیا جس کی قیادت محمد بن قاسم کر رہے تھے۔

                  جہاں داہر اپنی سلطنت کا دفاع کرتے ہوئے مر گیا۔

                  392

                  In Feb 2024, Maryam Nawaz Sharif became the _____ Chief Minister of Punjab province?

                  Correct Answer: 19th
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Maryam Nawaz Sharif became first woman & 19th  CM Punjab in Feb 2024

                  Predecessor: Moshin Naqvi

                  Party Name: PML-N

                  Note: Maryam Nawaz is the first woman to become Chief Minister of any province of Pakistan.

                  393

                  Former Prime Minister and PTI Chairman Imran Khan was arrested on which date?

                  Correct Answer: 9 May 2023
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  On 9 May 2023, former prime minister and PTI chairman Imran Khan was arrested in Al-Qadir Trust case.

                  Case pertains to NAB.


                  القادر ٹرسٹ کیس اس ساڑھے چار سو کنال سے زیادہ زمین کے عطیے سے متعلق ہے جو بحریہ ٹاؤن کی جانب سے القادر یونیورسٹی کے لیے دی گئی تھی۔


                  394

                  What is the total length of Indus River?

                  Correct Answer: 3180 km
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Indus River Length: 3,180 km

                  The Indus River starts in Tibet

                  The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

                  The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

                  The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

                  River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

                  The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

                  National River of Pakistan: Indus River

                  6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

                  Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

                  The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 

                  The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

                  ND11-2-2023**

                  ND13-1-2023
                  395

                  When Pakistan first time became the non-permanent member of Security Council?

                  Correct Answer: 1952
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan has become a non-permanent member of the Security Council Eight Times:-

                  1.      1952-1953,

                  2.      1968-1969,

                  3.      1976-1977,

                  4.      1983-1984,

                  5.      1993-1994,

                  6.      2003-2004,

                  7.       2012-2013

                  8.  2025-2026

                  • Permanent members of the UN Security Council are
                    • France
                    • China
                    • Uk
                    • USA 
                    • Russia
                    • *******
                    • The Security Council consists of 10 non-permanent members elected by the General Assembly for a term of two years.
                    • ***
                      • total UNO Member 193
                      • Headquarters: New York, United States
                      • Founded: October 24, 1945





                          396

                          Benazir Bhutto first sworn in as Prime Minister of Pakistan on _________?

                          Correct Answer: 2 December 1988
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Prime Minister

                          She was sworn in as the Prime Minister of Pakistan on 2 December 1988.

                          As Prime Minister first time from 1988 to 1990 

                           As Prime Minister second time from 1993 to 1996.

                          Farooq Laghari dissolved Benazir Bhutto's second government.

                          She was the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

                          "All about Benazir Bhutto and important question"

                          BB was Born on June 21, 1953, in Karachi

                          BB was Assassinated on December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi

                          BB Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007)

                          BB Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

                          BB Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977),

                          BB Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.

                          education :

                          Which Nursery School did Benazir Bhutto attend? Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.

                          Which college did Benazir Bhutto attend? Radcliffe College

                          Which University did Benazir Bhutto attend? University of Oxford,

                          *******

                          ND13-5-2023


                          397

                          Geneva Accords between Afghanistan and Pakistan for the withdrawal of USSR troops was concluded in _____?

                          Correct Answer: 1988
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Geneva Accords concerning the Soviet withdrawal from Afghanistan were concluded on April 14, 1988.

                          Signed by the Foreign Ministers of Afghanistan and Pakistan, with the United States and Soviet Union as guarantors, the agreement called for the full withdrawal of Soviet troops, an end to foreign interference, and the voluntary return of refugees. 

                          398

                          Islamabad was declared district in _____?

                          Correct Answer: 1981
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Islamabad was officially designated as a district on October 14, 1981, through a notification issued by the government of Pakistan.

                          This was part of administrative reforms to streamline governance in the federal capital.

                          Prior to 1981, Islamabad was part of Rawalpindi District.

                          399

                          Former Prime Minister Zulifqar Ali Bhutto was executed on _____?

                          Correct Answer: 4th April 1979
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was hanged to death on 4 April 1979 District Jail in Rawalpindi

                          Additional information 

                          He was the president of Pakistan from 20 December 1971 to 14 August 1973

                          He was the founder of the Pakistan People's Party

                          Nickname: Quaid-e-Awam ("the People's Leader")

                          He was Born on January 5, 1928, in Larkana

                          Resting place: Bhutto family mausoleum


                          400

                          The second general elections under the 1973 Constitution were held which brought 6th National Assembly into existence ______?

                          Correct Answer: 1977
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The first general elections under the 1973 Constitution were held in 1973.

                          The second general elections under the same constitution were held three years later in 1977.

                          This election brought the 6th National Assembly into existence.

                          401

                          Al-Tafseer Al-Kabir was written by ______?

                          Correct Answer: Fakhr al-Din al-Razi
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Al-Tafseer al-Kabir (Mafatih al-Ghayb) is a famous Quranic commentary written by Imam Fakhr al-Din al-Razi.

                          It is renowned for its depth in theology, philosophy, and rational discussions along with tafseer.

                          402

                          Hazrat Sheikh Ali Bin Usman Hajweri (Hazrat Data Ganj) came to Lahore during the reign of _____?

                          Correct Answer: Masood Ghaznavi
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Hazrat Sheikh Ali Bin Usman Hajweri (Hazrat Data Ganj) came to Lahore in 1041 AD with Masud Ghazni, the son of Mahmud, a Turko-Afghan invader. 

                          He was born in Ghazni, Afghanistan in 1009 AD.

                          He was a Persian Sufi mystic and Islamic scholar.

                          He wrote a famous book on mysticism.

                          He died in Lahore in 1072 AD at the age of 63.

                          403

                          The Tragedy of Chauri Chaura took place on _____?

                          Correct Answer: 4th February 1922
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Chauri Chaura incident, sometimes called a tragedy, took place on February 4, 1922.

                          During a protest as part of the Non-cooperation movement, a crowd clashed with police, leading to the police firing on the crowd. 

                          404

                          Indicate the highest civil award among the following: Tamgha-e-Shujaat, Sitara-e-Shujaat, Hilal-e-Shujaat, Nishan-e-Shujaat

                          Correct Answer: Nishan-e-Shujaat
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In Pakistan, Nishan is the highest rank among all civil and military awards.

                          Nishan-e-Shujaat is thus the highest civil award for bravery, above Hilal, Sitara, and Tamgha.

                          405

                          The ex-state of Amb is famous for _____?

                          Correct Answer: Natural Forests
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The ex-state of Amb (in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan) is famous for its natural forests.

                          These forests are mostly found in the Hazara region, rich in biodiversity and timber.

                          406

                          The power of the Legislative councils was increased by _______?

                          Correct Answer: Minto – Morley Reforms, 1909
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Indian Councils Act 1909 , also known as Minto-Morley Reforms.

                          Act of the Parliament of the United Kingdom that brought about a limited increase in the involvement of Indians in the governance of British India.

                          ***

                          The Morley-Minto Reforms became law in 1909 as the Indian Councils Act.

                          407

                          Abu-al-Fath Daud was the Ismaili ruler of ________?

                          Correct Answer: Multan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Abul Fateh Daud was the Fatimid Ismaili ruler of Multan.

                          He was deposed by Mahmud of Ghazni, who also massacred the Ismailis in the course of his conquest of Multan.

                          408

                          Under which article of 1973 Constitution is the Council of Common Interests (CCI) established?

                          Correct Answer: Article 153
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Prime Minister of Pakistan is the Chairman of the Council of Common Interests (CCI).

                          The CCI is a constitutional body established under Article 153 of the 1973 Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

                          The CCI's other members are:

                          • The four Provincial Chief Ministers
                          • Three members nominated by the Prime Minister, usually Cabinet members
                          409

                          Sindh was conquered by Muhammad bin Qasim during the _____ period.

                          Correct Answer: Umayyads
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Muhammad bin Qasim, a young Arab general, conquered Sindh (present-day Pakistan) in 712 CE during the Umayyad Caliphate (661-750 CE).

                          • The Governor General of India during the conquest of Sindh: Lord Ellenborough.
                          • Lord Ellenborough: He was the Governor General of India from 1842 to 1844 and oversaw the conquest of Sindh in 1843.
                          • Conquest of Sindh: Sindh was conquered by Charles Napier, who was the first Chief Commissioner and Governor of Sindh in 1947.


                          410

                          The SAARC movement was launched for?

                          Correct Answer: Regional cooperation
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          SAARC stands for South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation.

                          SAARC Secretariat was founded by Bangladeshi diplomat Abul Ahsan

                          The SAARC movement was launched for Regional cooperation

                          SAARC was Founded in Dhaka: on December 8, 1985

                          Headquarters: Kathmandu
                          *****

                          SAARC cooperation has given observer status to 9 states:

                          1. Australia,
                          2. China,
                          3. the European Union,
                          4. Iran,
                          5. Japan,
                          6. Mauritius,
                          7. Myanmar,
                          8. South Korea
                          9. and the United States

                          *******

                          There is a total of eight members of SAARC;

                          Remember Hints: MBBS PAIN
                          1. Afghanistan,
                          2. Bangladesh,
                          3. Bhutan,
                          4. India,
                          5. Nepal,
                          6. the Maldives,
                          7. Pakistan
                          8. and Sri Lanka
                          411

                          Partition of India Plan was announced on?

                          Correct Answer: 3 June 1947
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          On 3 June 1947 Lord Mountbatten announced the partition plan.

                          **

                          The 3rd June 1947 Plan was also known as the Mountbatten Plan.

                          The 3rd June Plan announced the partition of British India into 2 states:

                          • India
                          • Pakistan

                          Punjab and Bengal were divided in the 3rd June Plan.

                          The third June Plan was worked out by Lord Mountbatten.

                          ****

                          412

                          In which province Manchar lake is situated?

                          Correct Answer: Sindh
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Jamshoro Sindh Pakistan.

                          It collects water from the Kithara mountain range

                          and is one of South Asia's largest.

                          Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Pakistan.

                          Manchhar Lake covers an area of  23.5 km (14.6 mi)

                          Part of: Indus River basin

                          413

                          Noor Mahal is located in in which city of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Bahawalpur
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Noor Mahal is a Pakistan Army-owned palace in Bahawalpur.

                          It was built in 1872

                          The construction of the palace was completed in 1875

                          Note 

                          The palace was originally constructed to serve as a residence for the Nawabs of the Bahawalpur princely state during the British Raj era

                          414

                          What kind of power is produced by Ghazi Brotha?

                          Correct Answer: Hydroelectric Power
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Ghazi-Barotha Hydropower Project is a 1,450 MW un-of-the-river hydropower, connected to the Indus River.

                          Ghazi-Barotha  Location Between Attock, Punjab and Haripur, KP

                          Construction of the project began in 1995. 

                          Opening date: 2002

                          The Official name of Ghazi-Barotha Hydropower Project is غازى بروتھا

                          415

                          Who among the following translated CHACHNAMA from Arabic to Persian?

                          Correct Answer: Ali Kufi
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Chachnama takes its name from Raja Chach of Sindh, whose son Dahar stood against the Arabs (led by Muhammad bin Qasim).

                          The Chachnama was originally written by Kazi Ismail, the first Kazi of Alor appointed by Muhammad Qasim after the conquest of Sindh and translated into Persian by Ali bin Mohammad Kufi in 1224-26 A.D.

                          416

                          What is the height of K2, second highest mountain of world?

                          Correct Answer: 28251 feet
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          K-2 is Also Known As Godwin Austin

                          'K' standing for Karakoram.

                          Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.

                           after the world's highest peak Mount Everest.

                          Mount K2 is part of the Karakorum Range located on the border of Pakistan and China.

                          K2 height 8,611 meters

                          417

                          Mother of the nation of Pakistan?

                          Correct Answer: Fatima Jinnah
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Fatima Jinnah is referred to as Madr-e-Millat (Mother of Nation)

                          Fatima Jinnah: younger sister of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah

                          She was born: 30th July 1893

                          She died on 9 July 1967 at the age: of 71 in Karachi

                          Fatima Jinnah profession: Dental Surgeon


                          Additional Information


                          On 2 January 1965, Fatima Jinnah was a presidential candidate against Ayub Khan


                          There were four candidates: 

                          • Ayub Khan
                          • Fatima Jinnah
                          • and two obscure people with no party affiliation. 

                          Laltain was the election symbol of Fatima Jinnah 


                          Nominee
                          		Ayub Khan
                          		Fatima Jinnah
                          Party
                          		CML
                          		COP
                          Electoral vote
                          		49,951
                          		28,691
                          Percentage
                          		62.43%
                          		35.86%


                          418

                          The second constitution (1962) of Pakistan was enforced on?

                          Correct Answer: 8th June 1962
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1956, was promulgated on 23rd March 1956.

                          Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1962, was promulgated on 8th  June 1962.

                          Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973, was promulgated on 14th August 1973.

                          419

                          The Lahore session of Muslim League held in 1940 was presided over by_____?

                          Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          On March 23, A.K. Fazul Haq, the Chief Minister of Bengal, moved the historical Lahore Resolution

                          ********

                          Lahore Resolution Translation English to Urdu: Mulana Zafar Ali Khan

                          Lahore Resolution Presented by A.K Fazul Haq        

                          Participate From Punjab (Leader): Zafar Ali Khan

                          Participate From Sindh (Leader): Sir Abdullah Haroon

                          Participate From Balochistan (Leader): Qazi Faiz Isa

                          Participate From KPK (Leader): Sardar Aurangzaib Khan

                          CM Banglad : A.K Fazul Haq 

                          United Province: Khalid Zaman


                          AGM 08-01-2023


                          420

                          At the time when Khilafat delegation visited England in 1920, the British Prime Minister was _____?

                          Correct Answer: Lloyd George
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          In 1920, when the Khilafat Delegation visited England, the British Prime Minister was David Lloyd George.

                          He led Britain during and after World War I and dealt with issues related to the Ottoman Caliphate, which concerned the Khilafat leaders.

                          421

                          Simla Deputation (October 1906) comprised how many Muslim leaders?

                          Correct Answer: 35
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          The Simla Deputation of 1906 was made up of 35 Muslim leaders: 
                          Details
                          Who led the deputation? Aga Khan III
                          When did it take place? October 1, 1906
                          Where did it take place? Viceregal Lodge in Simla
                          Who met with the deputation? Lord Minto, the Viceroy of the British
                          What was the purpose? To demand separate electorates and secure Muslim interests

                          422

                          The Muslim Deputation met the Viceroy in 1906 in ____?

                          Correct Answer: Simla
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • Sir Agha Khan led the Simla Deputation.
                            • Indian Muslim leaders organized it.
                            • The delegation met the Governor-General and Viceroy, Lord Minto, in Simla on 1 October 1906.

                            شملہ وفد 1906

                            1. شملہ وفد یکم اکتوبر 1906 کو وائسرائے ہند سے ملا.

                            2. شملہ وفد کا صدر سر آغا خان تھا.

                            3. اس میں 35 لوگ / ارکان شامل تھے.

                            4. سب سے پہلے شملہ وفد ایم اے علی گڑھ کالج کے پرنسپل سے ملا.

                            5. پر. نسپل نے سیکرٹری وائسرائے کرنل ڈونلپ سے یہ میٹنگ طے کر دیا، ان ہی کےذریعے وفد وائسرائے لارڈ منٹو سے ملا.

                            6. چونکہ مسلمانوں کا یہ وفد وائسرائے ہند سے شملہ کے مقام پر ملا، اس وجہ سے اسکا نام شملہ وفد بن گیا.

                            423

                            When Simon Commission submitted its report?

                            Correct Answer: 1930
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            In 1930, the Commission published its two-volume report, also known as the Simon Report.

                            ·        The Indian Statutory Commission also known as Simon Commission,

                            ·        was a group of seven Members of Parliament

                            ·        under the chairmanship of Sir John Simon.

                            ·        The commission arrived in India in 1928

                            ·        to study constitutional reform in Britain's largest and most important possession.

                            Simon Commission wanted to check the working of Government of India Act, 1919.


                            424

                            In 1935 Sindh was Separated from Bombay due to _____?

                            Correct Answer: Simon Commission Report
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation

                            Sindh was separated from the Bombay Presidency in 1935 following recommendations influenced by the Simon Commission Report.

                            This separation led to the creation of Sindh as a separate province within British India.

                            425

                            Khilafat Movement was launched in the subcontinent in ______?

                            Correct Answer: 1919
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • The movement lasted from 1919 to 1924 AD
                            • Ended after the abolition of the Caliphate.
                            • It was a Religio-Political movement.
                            • It aimed to protect the Ottoman Caliphate after World War I.
                            • It was mainly led by the Ali Brothers.
                            • Major leaders were Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar and Shaukat Ali.
